content.text: I appreciate your perspective, so let's find a {{c1::middle ground}} that addresses both of our concerns.
content.explanation: middle ground is a common idiom meaning a compromise or a set of mutual concessions between two opposing viewpoints.
content.spanish: Aprecio tu perspectiva, así que busquemos un punto medio que aborde ambas preocupaciones.
content.usage:
Pattern: find a middle ground / reach a middle ground
  • After hours of debate, we finally found a middle ground.
  • It is difficult to reach a middle ground on this budget issue.

content.text: I understand your frustration, let's focus on finding a {{c1::middle ground}} that works for both teams.
content.explanation: A 'middle ground' is a compromise or position of agreement between two opposing viewpoints or parties.
content.spanish: Entiendo su frustración, enfoquémonos en encontrar un punto medio/acuerdo que funcione para ambos equipos.
content.usage:
Pattern: find a middle ground / compromise
  • Negotiations succeeded once both sides found a middle ground.
  • We need to establish a middle ground regarding work hours.

content.text: Let's schedule a call to {{c1::iron out}} these misunderstandings before the client meeting.
content.explanation: To 'iron out' is a phrasal verb meaning to resolve, smooth over, or eliminate minor problems or differences in views.
content.spanish: Agendemos una llamada para resolver/limar estas asperezas antes de la reunión con el cliente.
content.usage:
Pattern: iron out [problems / details / differences]
  • We need to iron out the details of the contract.
  • They met to iron out their political differences.

content.text: I want to make sure I fully grasp your point; could you please {{c1::elaborate on}} your concerns?
content.explanation: To 'elaborate on' means to add more details, explanation, or information to a statement that was previously brief.
content.spanish: Quiero asegurarme de entender completamente su punto; ¿podría por favor detallar/explayarse sobre sus preocupaciones?
content.usage:
Pattern: elaborate on [point / concerns / strategy]
  • Could you elaborate on how this database schema works?
  • The manager elaborated on the restructuring plan.

content.text: Let's keep this discussion objective and avoid {{c1::pointing fingers}} at individuals.
content.explanation: To 'point fingers' is an idiomatic expression meaning to accuse or place blame on someone else for a failure or problem.
content.spanish: Mantengamos esta discusión objetiva y evitemos buscar culpables/echarle la culpa a individuos.
content.usage:
Pattern: point fingers / play the blame game
  • Instead of pointing fingers, let's focus on fixing the bug.
  • She is always quick to point fingers when things go wrong.

content.text: I appreciate you bringing this up; let's work together to {{c1::address the root cause}} of this issue.
content.explanation: To 'address the root cause' means to identify and fix the underlying, fundamental reason why a problem occurred, rather than just treating symptoms.
content.spanish: Le agradezco que mencione esto; trabajemos juntos para abordar la causa raíz de este problema.
content.usage:
Pattern: address the root cause / resolve root issue
  • Rebooting the server only patches the symptom; we must address the root cause.
  • Our investigation aimed to address the root cause of the data breach.

content.text: We seem to have reached a {{c1::deadlock}}; let's bring in a neutral third party to mediate.
content.explanation: A 'deadlock' (or impasse) is a situation in a negotiation where no progress is possible because neither party is willing to compromise.
content.spanish: Parece que hemos llegado a un punto muerto/callejón sin salida; involucremos a un tercero neutral para mediar.
content.usage:
Pattern: reach a deadlock / break a deadlock
  • The contract talks ended in a complete deadlock.
  • We need a mediator to help us break the deadlock.

content.text: I agree that we need to act fast, but we cannot afford to {{c1::cut corners}} on safety.
content.explanation: To 'cut corners' is an idiom meaning to do something in the easiest, cheapest, or fastest way, often sacrificing quality or safety standards.
content.spanish: Estoy de acuerdo en que debemos actuar rápido, pero no podemos permitirnos tomar atajos/escatimar en seguridad.
content.usage:
Pattern: cut corners / skimp on quality
  • The builder cut corners and used cheap materials.
  • Never cut corners on database security audits.

content.text: Let's take a step back and look at the {{c1::bigger picture}} before making a decision.
content.explanation: The 'bigger picture' refers to the overall context, long-term goals, or broad view of a situation, rather than small details.
content.spanish: Demos un paso atrás y miremos el panorama completo/la perspectiva general antes de tomar una decisión.
content.usage:
Pattern: look at the bigger picture / overview
  • Don't get bogged down in detail; look at the bigger picture.
  • A manager must always focus on the bigger picture.

content.text: I apologize for the oversight; I will take full responsibility and {{c1::make it right}}.
content.explanation: To 'make it right' means to correct a mistake, repair a bad situation, or compensate someone for an error.
content.spanish: Pido disculpas por el descuido; asumiré toda la responsabilidad y lo corregiré/enmendaré.
content.usage:
Pattern: make it right / repair the damage
  • We sent the client a refund to make it right.
  • If you make a mistake, do your best to make it right.

content.text: Instead of focusing on past mistakes, let's look forward and find a constructive way to {{c1::move past this}}.
content.explanation: To 'move past' a problem means to accept what happened, stop arguing about it, and focus your energy on future progress.
content.spanish: En lugar de enfocarnos en los errores pasados, miremos hacia adelante y encontremos una manera constructiva de superar esto.
content.usage:
Pattern: move past this / overcome conflict
  • We had differences, but we managed to move past them.
  • Let's move past this misunderstanding and continue working.

content.text: I want to apologize if my comments came across as harsh; that was not my {{c1::intention}}.
content.explanation: 'Intention' refers to a person's aim, goal, or purpose behind an action or statement.
content.spanish: Quiero disculparme si mis comentarios parecieron duros; esa no era mi intención.
content.usage:
Pattern: have no intention / standard intention
  • It was not my intention to offend anyone.
  • We must clarify our intentions before signing the contract.

content.text: Let's schedule a facilitated workshop to resolve the friction between the development and product {{c1::teams}}.
content.explanation: In corporate settings, 'friction' or conflict commonly occurs between cross-functional teams (e.g. devs and product managers). Decoupling their workflows helps resolve this.
content.spanish: Agendemos un taller facilitado para resolver la fricción entre los equipos de desarrollo y de producto.
content.usage:
Pattern: friction between [teams] / resolve friction
  • We resolved the friction between dev and QA by aligning our goals.
  • Department silos often cause team friction.

content.text: We need to establish clear boundaries to prevent tasks from overlapping and causing future {{c1::friction}}.
content.explanation: 'Friction' refers to disagreement, conflict, or clash of wills between individuals or groups.
content.spanish: Necesitamos establecer límites claros para evitar que las tareas se traslapen y causen fricciones futuras.
content.usage:
Pattern: cause friction / reduce friction
  • Ambiguity in roles can cause friction in the workspace.
  • Clear boundaries help reduce team friction.

content.text: I agree that we have different approaches, but we must find a way to compromise and {{c1::collaborate effectively}}.
content.explanation: To 'collaborate effectively' means to work together productively, sharing goals and communicating openly to achieve a task successfully.
content.spanish: Estoy de acuerdo en que tenemos enfoques diferentes, pero debemos encontrar una forma de llegar a un acuerdo y colaborar de manera efectiva.
content.usage:
Pattern: collaborate effectively / team collaboration
  • Our team uses Slack to collaborate effectively from different locations.
  • We must collaborate effectively to launch on schedule.

content.text: Let's document the agreed-upon resolutions so we can refer back to them if issues {{c1::resurface}}.
content.explanation: To 'resurface' means to arise or become active again after a period of being hidden, resolved, or inactive.
content.spanish: Documentemos las resoluciones acordadas para que podamos consultarlas si los problemas resurgen/vuelven a aparecer.
content.usage:
Pattern: issues resurface / problems reappear
  • The database bugs resurfaced after the patch deployment.
  • We must act before past conflicts resurface.

content.text: I understand you have concerns about the new policy; let's discuss them openly in a {{c1::safe space}}.
content.explanation: A 'safe space' is a supportive, non-judgmental environment where people can speak openly about concerns or feelings without fear of retaliation.
content.spanish: Entiendo que tenga inquietudes sobre la nueva política; discutámoslas abiertamente en un espacio seguro.
content.usage:
Pattern: safe space / supportive environment
  • Our one-on-one calls are a safe space to share feedback.
  • We created a safe space for employees to report harassment.

content.text: We need to ensure that everyone feels heard and respected during this restructuring {{c1::process}}.
content.explanation: A 'restructuring process' involves reorganizing a company's hierarchy, teams, or operational flows, which often causes anxiety and conflict among staff.
content.spanish: Necesitamos asegurarnos de que todos se sientan escuchados y respetados durante este proceso de reestructuración.
content.usage:
Pattern: restructuring process / organizational change
  • The restructuring process led to several department merges.
  • We must communicate clearly during the restructuring process.

content.text: Instead of assuming bad intentions, let's give our teammates the {{c1::benefit of the doubt}}.
content.explanation: To give someone the 'benefit of the doubt' means to believe their statements or assume their actions are benign, even if you are not completely sure.
content.spanish: En lugar de asumir malas intenciones, demos a nuestros compañeros de trabajo el beneficio de la duda.
content.usage:
Pattern: give the benefit of the doubt
  • He was late, but I gave him the benefit of the doubt because of traffic.
  • Always give colleagues the benefit of the doubt before raising issues.

content.text: If we cannot reach an agreement, we will have to escalate this decision to the steering {{c1::committee}}.
content.explanation: A 'steering committee' is a advisory board of high-level stakeholders that guides a project and resolves high-level conflicts or deadlocks.
content.spanish: Si no podemos llegar a un acuerdo, tendremos que escalar esta decisión al comité de dirección.
content.usage:
Pattern: steering committee / governing board
  • The steering committee rejected the proposed budget increase.
  • Escalate the contract dispute to the steering committee.

content.scenario: Support Ops 🚨: Escalation Opening — Urgency Without Panic
content.text: To open an escalation call with authority but without panic, say: "{{c1::I'm escalating this because we've crossed the impact threshold where I need your support to resolve it quickly.}}"
content.explanation: This phrase does two things simultaneously: (1) it signals that escalation is the correct action (threshold crossed), not a sign of helplessness; (2) 'support to resolve quickly' frames the executive as an enabler, not a rescue. Avoid 'I don't know what to do' — it erodes confidence.
content.spanish: 'Lo escalo porque hemos cruzado el umbral de impacto donde necesito tu apoyo para resolverlo rápidamente.' — escalar es la acción correcta, no una señal de incapacidad.
content.usage:
• "This is a P1 for us — I'm escalating because the issue is cross-team and I need your authority to expedite the vendor response."
  • "I wouldn't escalate if I hadn't tried everything at my level. We need your weight behind this to unblock the infrastructure team."

content.scenario: Support Ops 🚨: Impact Statement in Escalation
content.text: Before escalating, quantify the impact with: "{{c1::This is affecting [N] customers / [$ revenue] / [SLA metric] since [time].}}"
content.explanation: Executives make priority decisions based on business impact, not technical severity. A 'database error' means nothing; 'affecting 3,400 enterprise users and $180K in at-risk renewals since 6 AM' makes priority clear. Always lead with who/what is affected, not with the technical cause.
content.spanish: 'Esto está afectando a [N] clientes / [$X en ingresos] / [métrica SLA] desde [hora].' — los ejecutivos priorizan por impacto de negocio, no por severidad técnica.
content.usage:
• "This is affecting approximately 2,100 active users in the US-West region and has been ongoing since 3:47 PM. SLA breach window opens in 90 minutes."
  • "Impact: two enterprise accounts that represent $240K ARR are unable to access core functionality. We've had 14 contacts in the last 2 hours."

content.scenario: Support Ops 🚨: What You've Already Tried
content.text: In an escalation, always state what you've ruled out with: "{{c1::We've already tried X, Y, and Z. None resolved it. The blocker is [specific issue].}}"
content.explanation: Executives and senior engineers will ask 'did you try X?' before helping. Pre-empting this question with a clear 'what we've done' list saves time and signals competence. It changes the conversation from 'let me fix your problem' to 'here's a clear picture — what do you know that I don't?'
content.spanish: 'Ya intentamos X, Y y Z. Ninguno lo resolvió. El bloqueador es [problema específico].' — anticiparse a las preguntas sobre lo intentado demuestra competencia y acelera la resolución.
content.usage:
• "We've already: restarted all pods, rolled back the last deployment, checked the database connections. Issue persists. The blocker is the upstream API that requires the vendor's ops team to cycle."
  • "I've tried three workarounds: cache bypass, direct DB query, and region failover. None worked. We need infrastructure admin access to proceed."

content.text: The gold standard for async escalation is the 3-line format: {{c1::Line 1: What broke. Line 2: Impact right now. Line 3: What I need from you.}}
content.explanation: When escalating via Slack, email, or paging, long messages get skimmed or missed. The 3-line format forces radical compression and ensures the reader knows immediately what action to take. This is the incident communication equivalent of BLUF. Every word after line 3 is optional context.
content.spanish: El estándar dorado para escalar de forma asíncrona: Línea 1: Qué se rompió. Línea 2: Impacto ahora mismo. Línea 3: Qué necesito de ti. Compresión radical para máxima claridad.
content.usage:
• Line 1: "Auth service returning 503s for all EU users since 08:22 UTC."
    Line 2: "~4,500 users can't log in. SLA breach in 38 minutes."
    Line 3: "Need @infra-team to check the load balancer config. Can you join the bridge call?"

content.scenario: Support Ops 🚨: Managing Customer During Escalation
content.text: While escalating internally, keep the customer stable with: "{{c1::I'm actively working on this with our team right now. I'll have an update for you in [X minutes/hours].}}"
content.explanation: The worst thing during an escalation is customer silence. 'Working on it' without a timeline creates anxiety. The time commitment — even if it's just a check-in update, not a resolution — gives the customer a predictable next touchpoint. Never say 'I don't know when this will be fixed.'
content.spanish: 'Estoy trabajando activamente en esto con nuestro equipo ahora mismo. Tendré una actualización para ti en [X minutos/horas].' — nunca dejes al cliente en silencio durante una escalada.
content.usage:
• "I've escalated this to our senior infrastructure team and I'm in a live call with them now. I'll have an update for you by 2:30 PM — that's 45 minutes from now."
  • "I'm not going to leave you hanging. Even if we don't have a fix, I'll update you every 30 minutes until we do."

content.text: After an escalation is resolved, close it with: "{{c1::A brief summary of what happened, what we did, and what we're putting in place to prevent it.}}"
content.explanation: The post-escalation debrief is often skipped in favor of moving on. This is a mistake. A structured close-out — cause, response, prevention — builds institutional trust, creates documentation for future incidents, and signals operational maturity. Keep it to 3-5 bullets, not a full report.
content.spanish: 'Un breve resumen de qué ocurrió, qué hicimos y qué ponemos en marcha para prevenirlo.' — el cierre post-escalada construye confianza institucional y documenta aprendizajes.
content.usage:
• "Quick close-out: root cause was a misconfigured load balancer rule pushed at 08:15. Resolution was a config rollback at 09:47. Prevention: adding automated config validation to the deployment pipeline by next Thursday."
  • "Full postmortem will follow, but the headline: we resolved it in 85 minutes, customer was kept informed throughout, and we've already opened 3 follow-up tickets for system hardening."

content.text: In support operations, escalation tiers map to: {{c1::L1 (first contact, standard playbook), L2 (technical deep-dive, specialist), L3 (engineering or vendor-level), and Executive (business impact decision needed)}}.
content.explanation: Understanding and naming escalation tiers correctly prevents both over-escalation (involving executives in L2 issues) and under-escalation (keeping L3 issues at L2 too long). Use tier language explicitly: 'I'm escalating to L3 because we've exhausted the L2 playbook' signals structured thinking to leadership.
content.spanish: Los niveles de escalada: L1 (contacto inicial, guión estándar), L2 (técnico especializado), L3 (ingeniería o proveedor), Ejecutivo (decisión de impacto de negocio). Nombrarlos correctamente previene la escalada excesiva.
content.usage:
• "This is an L3 escalation — we've been through the L2 runbook twice and need engineering to look at the source code."
  • "I'm keeping this at L2 for now — the specialist team has a lead. I'll escalate to executive if we don't have resolution in 2 hours."

content.scenario: Support Ops 🚨: Pushing Back on Inappropriate Escalation
content.text: When a customer escalates unnecessarily, de-escalate with: "{{c1::I understand this feels urgent. Let me assure you we're already treating this with the highest priority. Involving [name/team] at this stage would actually slow us down — here's why.}}"
content.explanation: Customers sometimes escalate to executives not because they need executive action, but because they feel unheard. This phrase validates the urgency feeling while making a logical case for why staying at the current level is faster. It redirects the focus from 'who's in charge' to 'what gets this solved fastest.'
content.spanish: 'Entiendo que esto se siente urgente. Déjame asegurarte que ya lo estamos tratando con la máxima prioridad. Involucrar a [nombre] en esta etapa realmente nos ralentizaría — aquí está el porqué.'
content.usage:
• "I hear you — this is critical for your business. I want to be transparent: our CTO joining the call right now would take us 30 minutes to set up. My team has the exact expertise needed and we're live on this right now."
  • "Escalating to the VP at this point would actually reset the clock. Let's give my team 90 more minutes with the authority I already have."

content.text: I want to ensure we are aligned, so I'd like to {{c1::address a pattern}} I've noticed in your recent deliverables.
content.explanation: address a pattern is a polite, constructive way to bring up a recurring issue in performance without making the employee feel immediately attacked.
content.spanish: Quiero asegurarme de que estemos alineados, por lo que me gustaría abordar un patrón que he notado en tus entregables recientes.
content.usage:
Pattern: address a pattern of [behavior]
  • We need to address a pattern of late arrivals in morning standups.
  • Thank you for addressing a pattern that was affecting our release schedule.

content.text: According to Kim Scott's Radical Candor framework, effective leadership requires balancing the two dimensions of {{c1::Care Personally}} (Cuidar Personalmente) and {{c2::Challenge Directly}} (Desafiar Directamente).
content.explanation: Failing to challenge directly while caring personally leads to 'Empatía Ruinosa' (Ruinous Empathy), which prevents growth. Challenging directly without caring leads to 'Agresión Ofensiva' (Obnoxious Aggression).
content.spanish: Según el marco de Sinceridad Radical de Kim Scott, el liderazgo efectivo requiere equilibrar las dos dimensiones de Cuidar Personalmente y Desafiar Directamente.
content.usage: Radical Candor is the 'sweet spot' where you care enough about someone to tell them the uncomfortable truth to help them grow.

content.text: During the 1-on-1 meeting, the manager noted: "While your code is robust, there is {{c1::room for improvement}} in your documentation. {{c2::Going forward}}, let's focus on adding README guides."
content.explanation: Room for improvement is a polite, constructive way to say that something needs development. **Going forward** is a transition phrase meaning 'in the future'.
content.spanish: Durante la reunión 1 a 1, el gerente señaló: "Aunque tu código es robusto, hay margen de mejora en tu documentación. En el futuro, concentrémonos en agregar guías README".
content.usage:
Pattern: room for improvement in [area] / going forward, we should [action]
  • There is room for improvement in our test coverage.
  • Going forward, all code must be reviewed by two peers.

content.text: I appreciate your hard work on this project; however, there is still {{c1::room for improvement}} in your documentation.
content.explanation: 'Room for improvement' is a polite, professional idiom meaning that something is not perfect and has potential to be better.
content.spanish: Aprecio su arduo trabajo en este proyecto; sin embargo, todavía hay margen de mejora en su documentación.
content.usage:
Pattern: room for improvement / areas of development
  • The report was good, but there's room for improvement.
  • There is always room for improvement in software performance.

content.text: To help you grow, I want to offer some {{c1::constructive feedback}} on your client presentation.
content.explanation: 'Constructive feedback' is supportive advice or critique designed to help someone improve their work or behavior, rather than simply pointing out faults.
content.spanish: Para ayudarle a crecer, quiero ofrecerle retroalimentación constructiva sobre su presentación con el cliente.
content.usage:
Pattern: constructive feedback / constructive criticism
  • I always appreciate constructive feedback from my manager.
  • Try to phrase your comments as constructive feedback.

content.text: You did a fantastic job managing the client's expectations; {{c1::keep up the good work}}!
content.explanation: 'Keep up the good work' is a common encouraging phrase used to praise someone's performance and motivate them to continue performing at a high level.
content.spanish: Hizo un trabajo fantástico gestionando las expectativas del cliente; ¡siga así (continúe con el buen trabajo)!
content.usage:
Pattern: keep up the good work / keep it up
  • The manager told us to keep up the good work.
  • Fantastic results this quarter, keep up the good work!

content.text: I've noticed that you've been missing deadlines; is there anything {{c1::holding you back}}?
content.explanation: To 'hold someone back' is a phrasal verb meaning to prevent someone from making progress, developing, or performing to their potential.
content.spanish: He notado que ha estado perdiendo fechas límite; ¿hay algo que le impida avanzar/le esté frenando?
content.usage:
Pattern: hold [someone] back / block progress
  • What is holding you back from applying for the promotion?
  • Lack of funding is holding the research back.

content.text: Let's set up a weekly check-in to track your progress and address any {{c1::pain points}} early.
content.explanation: A 'pain point' is a specific problem, obstacle, or difficulty experienced by employees, customers, or users.
content.spanish: Establezcamos una reunión de control semanal para realizar un seguimiento de su progreso y abordar cualquier punto crítico/dificultad temprano.
content.usage:
Pattern: address pain points / resolve customer pain points
  • The new software addresses the main pain points of our team.
  • We need to identify user pain points in the checkout flow.

content.text: Your technical skills are excellent, but you need to work on your {{c1::active listening}} during team meetings.
content.explanation: 'Active listening' is a communication technique that requires the listener to fully concentrate, understand, respond, and remember what is being said.
content.spanish: Sus habilidades técnicas son excelentes, pero necesita trabajar en su escucha activa durante las reuniones de equipo.
content.usage:
Pattern: practice active listening / active listening skills
  • Active listening is essential for effective conflict resolution.
  • She showed active listening by summarizing my points.

content.text: I want to recognize your effort; your dedication has made a {{c1::huge impact on}} the team's morale.
content.explanation: To 'make a huge impact on' means to have a very large, significant, or powerful effect or influence on a situation, group, or project.
content.spanish: Quiero reconocer su esfuerzo; su dedicación ha tenido un gran impacto en la moral del equipo.
content.usage:
Pattern: make a huge impact on [something]
  • The training made a huge impact on sales performance.
  • Your feedback has had a huge impact on my career growth.

content.text: I've noticed that you've been working late; please make sure you are balancing your workload to {{c1::avoid burnout}}.
content.explanation: To 'avoid burnout' means managing tasks, hours, and stress levels to prevent physical or mental collapse caused by overwork.
content.spanish: He notado que ha estado trabajando hasta tarde; por favor, asegúrese de equilibrar su carga de trabajo para evitar el agotamiento.
content.usage:
Pattern: avoid burnout / combat workplace stress
  • Taking regular breaks is crucial to avoid burnout.
  • Managers should monitor team workload to avoid burnout.

content.text: Your technical explanation was very clear, but you need to adapt your language when presenting to non-technical {{c1::audiences}}.
content.explanation: A 'non-technical audience' refers to clients, managers, or users who do not possess deep engineering or scientific knowledge, requiring simple language.
content.spanish: Su explicación técnica fue muy clara, pero debe adaptar su lenguaje al presentarse ante audiencias no técnicas.
content.usage:
Pattern: non-technical audience / business stakeholders
  • Explain the algorithm simply for a non-technical audience.
  • The director is a non-technical audience, so skip the code snippets.

content.text: I appreciate your initiative in organizing the files; it has saved the team a lot of {{c1::time}}.
content.explanation: To 'save time' means executing tasks efficiently to reduce the duration or effort required to complete a process.
content.spanish: Agradezco su iniciativa al organizar los archivos; le ha ahorrado mucho tiempo al equipo.
content.usage:
Pattern: save time / time savings
  • The automation script saved the team hours of manual work.
  • We optimized the query to save execution time.

content.text: To help you grow, let's identify some training courses that can help you build your {{c1::leadership skills}}.
content.explanation: 'Leadership skills' are the strengths and abilities individuals demonstrate when guiding processes, directing initiatives, and mentoring teammates.
content.spanish: Para ayudarle a crecer, identifiquemos algunos cursos de capacitación que puedan ayudarle a desarrollar sus habilidades de liderazgo.
content.usage:
Pattern: build leadership skills / leadership qualities
  • Effective communication is a core leadership skill.
  • The seminar focused on developing leadership skills in project managers.

content.text: Your coding standards are excellent, but you should try to be more proactive in code {{c1::reviews}}.
content.explanation: A 'code review' is the software development process where engineers inspect one another's source code to catch bugs and verify quality.
content.spanish: Sus estándares de codificación son excelentes, pero debería intentar ser más proactivo en las revisiones de código.
content.usage:
Pattern: code reviews / peer review
  • Be constructive when offering comments during code reviews.
  • We require two approvals on code reviews before merging.

content.text: I want to encourage you to share your ideas during brainstorming sessions; your perspective is {{c1::highly valued}}.
content.explanation: To be 'highly valued' means that a person's ideas, work, or presence is held in very high esteem and considered extremely important.
content.spanish: Quiero animarle a compartir sus ideas durante las sesiones de lluvia de ideas; su perspectiva es muy valorada.
content.usage:
Pattern: highly valued / valued team member
  • Your contribution to the database migration was highly valued.
  • She is a highly valued member of our division.

content.text: Let's work on improving your communication style to ensure updates are delivered in a timely {{c1::manner}}.
content.explanation: To deliver updates in a 'timely manner' (or timely fashion) means doing so quickly enough to be useful, before deadlines or issues arise.
content.spanish: Trabajemos en mejorar su estilo de comunicación para asegurar que las actualizaciones se entreguen de manera oportuna.
content.usage:
Pattern: in a timely manner / timely fashion
  • Please submit the report in a timely manner.
  • The team resolved the incident in a timely manner.

content.text: You did a great job handling that difficult client request; your patience was {{c1::exemplary}}.
content.explanation: 'Exemplary' is an adjective meaning serving as a desirable model; representing the best of its kind, worthy of imitation.
content.spanish: Hizo un gran trabajo manejando esa difícil solicitud del cliente; su paciencia fue ejemplar.
content.usage:
Pattern: exemplary behavior / exemplary work
  • Her dedication to quality is exemplary.
  • The audit praised the team's exemplary record-keeping.

content.text: I'd like to see you take on more responsibility in coordinating tasks for our next product {{c1::release}}.
content.explanation: A 'product release' is the distribution of a new or upgraded software application or feature set to users.
content.spanish: Me gustaría verle asumir más responsabilidad en la coordinación de tareas para nuestro próximo lanzamiento de producto.
content.usage:
Pattern: product release / software release / version release
  • We are patching bugs before the final product release.
  • The marketing campaign is aligned with the product release date.

content.text: I want to recognize your contribution to the team's success; your dedication has not gone {{c1::unnoticed}}.
content.explanation: To 'not go unnoticed' is a common professional idiom used to reassure an employee that managers have observed and appreciated their hard work.
content.spanish: Quiero reconocer su contribución al éxito del equipo; su dedicación no ha pasado inadvertida.
content.usage:
Pattern: not go unnoticed / recognized effort
  • Your extra hours during the database outage did not go unnoticed.
  • Her efforts to improve team morale have not gone unnoticed.

content.text: When a client objects to a price increase, you can say: "I hear your {{c1::concerns regarding the cost}}; however, this new tier {{c2::delivers significant value by}} doubling your API rate limit."
content.explanation: Acknowledging the objection first shows respect, while 'delivers value by' steers the conversation toward benefits rather than expense.
content.spanish: Cuando un cliente se opone a un aumento de precio, puedes decir: "Escucho sus inquietudes con respecto al costo; sin embargo, este nuevo nivel ofrece un valor significativo al duplicar su límite de velocidad de API".
content.usage:
Pattern: I hear your concerns regarding [topic]; however, this [solution] delivers value by [benefit]
  • I hear your concerns regarding the timeline; however, this phase delivers value by securing your data early.
  • We believe this update delivers significant value by reducing server load.

content.text: If a user rejects a solution, offer options: "If that option doesn't {{c1::align with your objectives}}, we can {{c2::explore a phased roll-out}} instead."
content.explanation: Align with objectives means to match their goals. **Explore a phased roll-out** means to implement the solution in gradual steps rather than all at once.
content.spanish: Si un usuario rechaza una solución, ofrece opciones: "Si esa opción no se alinea con sus objetivos, podemos explorar una implementación gradual en su lugar".
content.usage:
Pattern: If [solution] doesn't align with your objectives, we can explore [alternative]
  • If the standard plan doesn't align with your objectives, we can explore a custom contract.
  • Let's explore a phased roll-out of the security policy.

content.text: To uncover the root of a client's hesitation: "What is your {{c1::primary reservation about}} moving forward with this integration?"
content.explanation: Primary reservation refers to the main doubt, worry, or objection holding someone back from making a decision.
content.spanish: Para descubrir la raíz de la vacilación de un cliente: "¿Cuál es su principal reserva sobre seguir adelante con esta integración?"
content.usage:
Pattern: What is your primary reservation about [action]?
  • What is your primary reservation about migrating to the cloud?
  • His primary reservation about the project was the tight deadline.

content.text: When a customer compares you to a competitor: "I understand that [Competitor] has {{c1::a lower price point}}, but our platform provides {{c2::enterprise-grade security}} that is not offered elsewhere."
content.explanation: Lower price point is a professional euphemism for 'cheaper'. **Enterprise-grade** denotes high-quality, robust features suited for large, demanding organizations.
content.spanish: Cuando un cliente te compara con un competidor: "Entiendo que [Competidor] tiene un precio más bajo, pero nuestra plataforma ofrece seguridad de nivel empresarial que no se ofrece en otros lugares".
content.usage:
Pattern: understand [competitor] has a lower price point, but our [product] provides [advantage]
  • I understand they have a lower price point, but our service provides 99.99% uptime.
  • We need enterprise-grade security to satisfy compliance requirements.

content.text: I understand your concern about the budget, but let me show you how this tool will {{c1::save you money}} in the long run.
content.explanation: To 'save money' means to reduce expenses or prevent financial waste, a key value proposition in professional sales.
content.spanish: Entiendo su preocupación sobre el presupuesto, pero permítame mostrarle cómo esta herramienta le ahorrará dinero a largo plazo.
content.usage:
Pattern: save money / cost savings
  • Moving to the cloud will save us money on infrastructure.
  • This energy-efficient device will save you money.

content.text: That's a valid point; however, our software integrates seamlessly with your {{c1::legacy systems}}.
content.explanation: A 'legacy system' is an old or outdated computer system, technology, or software application that a company continues to use because it is critical to their business.
content.spanish: Ese es un punto válido; sin embargo, nuestro software se integra a la perfección con sus sistemas heredados/antiguos.
content.usage:
Pattern: legacy systems / legacy technology
  • Replacing legacy systems can be extremely expensive and risky.
  • We built an API wrapper to connect our app to their legacy system.

content.text: To address your security concerns, we are fully compliant with all major {{c1::industry standards}}.
content.explanation: An 'industry standard' is a technical specification, requirement, or guideline widely accepted and followed in a specific field (e.g. ISO 27001, SOC 2).
content.spanish: Para abordar sus preocupaciones de seguridad, cumplimos plenamente con todos los principales estándares de la industria.
content.usage:
Pattern: industry standards / compliance standards
  • Our security protocols meet the highest industry standards.
  • We must update our process to keep up with changing industry standards.

content.text: I hear what you are saying, but our customer support team is available {{c1::around the clock}} to help you.
content.explanation: 'Around the clock' is an idiom meaning continuously, 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
content.spanish: Escucho lo que dice, pero nuestro equipo de soporte al cliente está disponible las 24 horas del día para ayudarle.
content.usage:
Pattern: around the clock / 24/7 support
  • The engineering team worked around the clock to fix the database.
  • We offer customer service around the clock.

content.text: We can adjust the project scope to fit your timeline if that is the {{c1::deciding factor}} for you.
content.explanation: The 'deciding factor' is the key element, reason, or condition that makes a person resolve a choice or make a final decision.
content.spanish: Podemos ajustar el alcance del proyecto para adaptarlo a su cronograma si ese es el factor decisivo para usted.
content.usage:
Pattern: deciding factor / deal-breaker
  • Price was the deciding factor in our choice of vendor.
  • Safety features were the deciding factor when buying the car.

content.text: Let me walk you through our case studies to show how we solved similar problems for {{c1::other clients}}.
content.explanation: A 'case study' is a detailed analysis of a project or campaign, used as marketing evidence to show potential clients how a company successfully delivered value.
content.spanish: Permítame guiarle a través de nuestros estudios de caso para mostrar cómo resolvimos problemas similares para otros clientes.
content.usage:
Pattern: case study / client success story
  • We published three new case studies on our website.
  • Our case studies prove that our algorithm improves conversion rates.

content.text: I understand that our service is more expensive, but it includes features that our competitors charge {{c1::extra for}}.
content.explanation: To charge 'extra for [something]' means requiring additional payment for specific premium add-ons or services.
content.spanish: Entiendo que nuestro servicio es más costoso, pero incluye características por las que nuestros competidores cobran un cargo extra.
content.usage:
Pattern: charge extra for [add-on]
  • Many airlines charge extra for checked bags.
  • We do not charge extra for technical support.

content.text: To address your concerns about downtime, we offer a service level agreement (SLA) with a 99.9% {{c1::uptime guarantee}}.
content.explanation: An 'uptime guarantee' is a contractual promise by a hosting or software vendor specifying the minimum percentage of time a service will be operational.
content.spanish: Para abordar sus preocupaciones sobre el tiempo de inactividad, ofrecemos un acuerdo de nivel de servicio (SLA) con una garantía de tiempo de actividad del 99.9%.
content.usage:
Pattern: uptime guarantee / guaranteed uptime
  • Our cloud provider offers a 99.99% uptime guarantee.
  • Violating the uptime guarantee results in service credits for the client.

content.text: Our onboarding process is designed to minimize friction; we will guide your team through every {{c1::step}}.
content.explanation: A 'step' is a single action or phase in a sequence of events designed to achieve a goal (like onboarding).
content.spanish: Nuestro proceso de incorporación está diseñado para minimizar la fricción; guiaremos a su equipo a través de cada paso.
content.usage:
Pattern: step by step / sequential steps
  • Follow the installation steps carefully.
  • What is the next step in our database migration?

content.text: I appreciate your concern about the implementation timeline, but our dedicated support team will handle the {{c1::heavy lifting}}.
content.explanation: 'Heavy lifting' is an idiom meaning the most difficult, labor-intensive, or complex part of a task or project.
content.spanish: Agradezco su preocupación sobre el cronograma de implementación, pero nuestro equipo de soporte dedicado se encargará del trabajo pesado.
content.usage:
Pattern: do the heavy lifting / handle heavy lifting
  • Our scripts do the heavy lifting of parsing logs automatically.
  • The consultant handled the heavy lifting during corporate restructuring.

content.text: We can start with a small pilot project to demonstrate the value of our tool before you commit to a {{c1::full contract}}.
content.explanation: A 'pilot project' (or pilot study) is a small-scale, short-term experiment conducted to evaluate feasibility, cost, and utility before full deployment.
content.spanish: Podemos comenzar con un pequeño proyecto piloto para demostrar el valor de nuestra herramienta antes de que se comprometa con un contrato completo.
content.usage:
Pattern: pilot project / pilot study / trial run
  • We launched a pilot project with three departments first.
  • The pilot project was highly successful, leading to full contract approval.

content.text: If safety is your main concern, our data servers are protected by military-grade encryption and regular {{c1::audits}}.
content.explanation: An 'audit' is an official inspection of an organization's accounts, security status, or processes, typically conducted by an independent body.
content.spanish: Si la seguridad es su principal preocupación, nuestros servidores de datos están protegidos por cifrado de grado militar y auditorías regulares.
content.usage:
Pattern: security audits / regular compliance audits
  • Our security protocols are verified by annual third-party audits.
  • We must prepare the documents for the financial audit next month.

content.text: We have client testimonials from companies in your industry showing how they improved efficiency by {{c1::40%}}.
content.explanation: A 'testimonial' is a formal statement testifying to someone's character, qualifications, or the value of a product/service, written by a satisfied customer.
content.spanish: Tenemos testimonios de clientes de empresas en su industria que muestran cómo mejoraron la eficiencia en un 40%.
content.usage:
Pattern: client testimonials / customer reviews
  • The sales deck includes several testimonials from enterprise clients.
  • Client testimonials help build trust with prospective buyers.

content.text: I hear what you are saying about resource constraints; that is why our tool requires almost zero {{c1::maintenance}}.
content.explanation: 'Maintenance' refers to the process of preserving a system, software, or building in good working condition through regular checks and repairs.
content.spanish: Escucho lo que dice sobre las limitaciones de recursos; es por eso que nuestra herramienta requiere casi cero mantenimiento.
content.usage:
Pattern: zero maintenance / low maintenance / routine maintenance
  • Our SaaS platform handles updates automatically, ensuring zero maintenance for you.
  • Database index maintenance is scheduled weekly.

content.text: We offer flexible contract terms that allow you to scale down your usage if your business needs {{c1::change}}.
content.explanation: To 'change' is the verb meaning to make or become different, commonly used to describe shifting business requirements.
content.spanish: Ofrecemos condiciones de contrato flexibles que le permiten reducir su uso si las necesidades de su negocio cambian.
content.usage:
Pattern: needs change / plans change
  • Our database schema must adapt as user needs change.
  • Business plans changed due to the economic downturn.

content.text: Let's schedule a demo session next week so your team can test the interface and see how {{c1::user-friendly}} it is.
content.explanation: 'User-friendly' describes an interface, software, or device that is easy to learn, understand, and navigate for consumers.
content.spanish: Programemos una sesión de demostración la próxima semana para que su equipo pueda probar la interfaz y ver qué tan fácil de usar es.
content.usage:
Pattern: user-friendly interface / user-friendly design
  • The new website layout is much more user-friendly.
  • We prioritized user-friendly navigation in our mobile app.

content.text: To open an incident bridge call efficiently, say: "{{c1::This is [name], incident commander. We have a [P1/P2] incident. Here's what we know: [2 sentences]. Our working hypothesis is [X]. I need [team A] on diagnosis and [team B] on customer communication. Let's go.}}"
content.explanation: Incident bridge calls without structure waste the first 10 minutes on 'who's on the call' and 'what do we know.' The incident commander role, explicit team assignments, and a working hypothesis cut that down to 60 seconds. 'Let's go' is a signal to begin parallel workstreams — not more discussion.
content.spanish: 'Soy [nombre], incident commander. Tenemos un incidente [P1/P2]. Esto es lo que sabemos: [2 oraciones]. Hipótesis de trabajo: [X]. Necesito a [equipo A] en diagnóstico y a [equipo B] en comunicación al cliente. Vamos.' — apertura de bridge call sin pérdida de tiempo.
content.usage: • "This is Carlos, IC. P1 — auth service down for EU region since 08:22. 4,500 users affected. Working hypothesis: load balancer misconfiguration from the 08:15 deployment. Engineering: dig into the LB config. Support: draft customer comms now. Let's go."

content.text: During an active incident, the standard update cadence is: {{c1::every 15-30 minutes for internal stakeholders, every 30-60 minutes for affected customers}}, with each update including: status, latest finding, next action.
content.explanation: Inconsistent updates during incidents create anxiety and trigger unnecessary escalations. Setting an explicit cadence — 'I'll update every 30 minutes until resolved' — prevents executives from interrupting the response team to check status. Each update must answer: where are we, what did we just learn, what happens next.
content.spanish: Durante un incidente activo, la cadencia de actualizaciones estándar es: cada 15-30 min para stakeholders internos, cada 30-60 min para clientes afectados. Cada actualización: estado, último hallazgo, próxima acción.
content.usage:
• "Update at 09:45: Still investigating. We've confirmed the issue is isolated to the authentication microservice. Next: Engineering is doing a config rollback. Next update: 10:15."
  • Template: "[Time] Update: Status [Open/Contained/Resolved]. Latest: [finding]. Next: [action]. ETA for next update: [time]."

content.text: A customer-facing incident statement must contain: {{c1::what is affected (not what broke technically), how many users, when it started, and an expected restoration time or next update time}}.
content.explanation: External incident communications are NOT technical troubleshooting notes. Customers don't need to know it's a 'LB misconfiguration' — they need to know 'EU login is unavailable and you can expect an update by 10:30 AM.' Oversharing technical details creates liability and confusion; undersharing creates panic and churn.
content.spanish: Una declaración de incidente dirigida al cliente debe contener: qué está afectado (no la causa técnica), cuántos usuarios, cuándo comenzó y un tiempo de restauración esperado o próxima actualización. No compartir detalles técnicos en comunicaciones externas.
content.usage:
• Good: "We are currently experiencing an issue affecting login for users in the EU region. Our team is actively working to restore service. We expect to provide a next update by 10:30 AM UTC. We apologize for the inconvenience."
  • Avoid: "Our load balancer threw a 502 due to a bad deployment at 08:15." (Too technical, implies fault.)

content.text: In incident management, P1 means {{c1::complete service outage or critical data loss with immediate customer impact}}, P2 means degraded service affecting many users, P3 means minor impact or workaround available.
content.explanation: Consistent severity language prevents 'everything is P1' fatigue. When severity is inflated, response teams can't prioritize. When it's deflated, executives are caught off guard. The ITIL/PagerDuty standard defines P1 as requiring immediate mobilization, 24/7 response, and executive notification. P2 requires a response within the business day.
content.spanish: P1: interrupción completa del servicio o pérdida crítica de datos con impacto inmediato al cliente. P2: servicio degradado que afecta a muchos usuarios. P3: impacto menor o hay solución alternativa. La coherencia en el lenguaje de severidad previene la fatiga de 'todo es P1'.
content.usage:
• P1: "Payment processing is completely down. Zero transactions completing. Mobilizing full response team now."
  • P2: "API response times are 4x above baseline for 20% of users. Degraded but functional. Investigating."
  • P3: "One report module is returning stale data. Workaround available. Fix scheduled for next sprint."

content.text: In a war room or bridge call, apply the rule: {{c1::speak only when you have a confirmed finding, a decision to make, or an action to assign — not to think out loud}}.
content.explanation: War room noise is one of the biggest time wasters during P1 incidents. When 10 people are speculating simultaneously, the signal-to-noise ratio collapses. The incident commander's job is to enforce structured communication: 'Do you have a finding or are you still investigating?' Thinking out loud belongs in side channels (Slack thread), not the main bridge.
content.spanish: En un war room o bridge call: habla solo cuando tengas un hallazgo confirmado, una decisión que tomar o una acción que asignar — no para pensar en voz alta. El IC debe imponer comunicación estructurada.
content.usage:
• IC: "Engineering — do you have something confirmed?" Eng: "Yes. The LB config shows rule #14 was modified at 08:13." IC: "Good. Can you revert it? How long?" Eng: "10 minutes." IC: "Go. I'll update stakeholders."
  • Avoid: Open speculation on the bridge call. Move hypotheses to a side Slack thread.

content.text: When declaring an incident resolved, use: "{{c1::As of [time], the incident is resolved. Service is fully restored for all users. Root cause: [brief]. Full postmortem within [timeframe].}}"
content.explanation: A clean resolution announcement closes the loop for all stakeholders simultaneously. The four elements — time, scope of restoration, brief root cause, postmortem commitment — give customers confidence that the issue is truly over and that the organization is learning from it. Never declare resolved without confirming with monitoring data.
content.spanish: 'A partir de [hora], el incidente está resuelto. El servicio está completamente restaurado para todos los usuarios. Causa raíz: [breve]. Postmortem completo en [plazo].' — cierra el ciclo para todos los stakeholders.
content.usage:
• "As of 10:52 AM UTC, the authentication service is fully restored for all EU users. Root cause: a load balancer config change at 08:13 was reverted. We'll publish a full postmortem within 5 business days."
  • Internal: "Incident closed. Full details in the incident ticket #INC-2341. Postmortem call scheduled for Thursday."

content.text: In incident retrospectives, avoid blame language. Replace 'John forgot to...' with {{c1::'The process didn't have a safeguard for...' or 'The system allowed X to happen without a check.'}}
content.explanation: Blameless postmortems (pioneered by Google SRE and Etsy) identify systemic failures, not individual failures. When a human makes a mistake, the blameless question is: 'What would have prevented this system from allowing that mistake?' Blame kills psychological safety, which reduces future incident reporting — making the system more fragile.
content.spanish: En las retrospectivas de incidentes, evita el lenguaje de culpa. Reemplaza 'John olvidó...' con 'El proceso no tenía una salvaguarda para...' Los postmortems sin culpa identifican fallas sistémicas, no individuales.
content.usage:
• Blame: "John deployed without testing."
  • Blameless: "The deployment pipeline didn't require a mandatory test gate before production push."
  • Blame: "Sarah missed the alert."
  • Blameless: "The alerting threshold was set too high, resulting in a delayed notification."

content.text: When paging a stakeholder mid-incident, the message must be under {{c1::5 lines}}, contain the severity level, impact, and one explicit ask.
content.explanation: Executives paged during incidents often receive walls of text or vague 'call me' messages. The sub-5-line constraint forces prioritization. The explicit ask ('can you join the bridge?' / 'I need your sign-off to engage vendor support') removes ambiguity. The recipient should know in 10 seconds whether to drop what they're doing.
content.spanish: Al enviar un mensaje de alerta a un ejecutivo durante un incidente: máximo 5 líneas, incluye el nivel de severidad, el impacto y una solicitud explícita. El receptor debe saber en 10 segundos si necesita actuar.
content.usage:
• "@CTO — P1 ACTIVE. Auth service down for 4,500 EU users since 08:22. SLA breach in 45 min. I need your authorization to engage [Vendor] premium support. Can you reply Y/N?"
  • "[Name] — P2 incident on payment processing. Degraded, not down. No exec action needed yet. I'll update you at 11:00."

content.text: I want to give you a brief {{c1::incident update}} on the current status of the outage.
content.explanation: An incident update is a concise communication about what is happening, who is involved, and what steps are being taken.
content.spanish: Quiero darles una breve actualización sobre el estado actual del incidente.
content.usage:
Pattern: incident update / status update
  • We sent an incident update to all affected users.
  • The incident update confirmed that the issue was contained.

content.text: The issue is currently affecting a small number of {{c1::affected users}} in the EU region.
content.explanation: Are the people who are experiencing the problem or disruption
content.spanish: El problema está afectando actualmente a un pequeño número de usuarios afectados en la región de la UE.
content.usage:
Pattern: affected users / impacted users
  • We are notifying affected users now.
  • Only a subset of impacted users is currently affected.

content.text: Our first priority is {{c1::containment}} so the issue does not expand to more customers.
content.explanation: Is the immediate action taken to limit the impact of an incident
content.spanish: Nuestra primera prioridad es contener el problema para que no se extienda a más clientes.
content.usage:
Pattern: containment / mitigate impact
  • The containment step was successful.
  • We are mitigating the impact as quickly as possible.

content.text: We have identified the {{c1::root cause}} and we are validating the fix in staging.
content.explanation: The root cause is the underlying reason why the problem happened.
content.spanish: Hemos identificado la causa raíz y estamos validando la solución en staging.
content.usage:
Pattern: root cause / underlying issue
  • The root cause was a failed deployment.
  • We are validating the underlying issue now.

content.text: We are taking steps to {{c1::restore trust}} by being transparent and proactive.
content.explanation: Restoring trust means rebuilding confidence after a service problem or mistake.
content.spanish: Estamos tomando medidas para recuperar la confianza siendo transparentes y proactivos.
content.usage:
Pattern: restore trust / rebuild confidence
  • Transparent communication helps restore trust.
  • The team is rebuilding confidence with customers.

content.text: Could you please {{c1::power cycle}} the router by unplugging it for 30 seconds?
content.explanation: power cycle is a technical verb meaning to turn an electronic device completely off (usually by cutting power) and back on again to reset it.
content.spanish: ¿Podrías por favor apagar y encender el router desenchufándolo durante 30 segundos?
content.usage:
Pattern: power cycle [device] (e.g. power cycle the server, power cycle the modem)
  • If your connection drops, try power cycling the access point.
  • I power cycled the switch, which solved the IP conflict.

content.text: We are currently experiencing a {{c1::service disruption}} affecting our primary database, and our team is working to mitigate it.
content.explanation: service disruption is the formal corporate term for an outage or downtime. mitigate means to reduce the severity or impact of an ongoing incident.
content.spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción del servicio que afecta a nuestra base de datos principal, y nuestro equipo está trabajando para mitigarlo.
content.usage:
Pattern: experience a service disruption | mitigate [incident/impact/risk]
  • The network migration caused a minor service disruption for 10 minutes.
  • We are deploying a hotfix to mitigate the database CPU spikes.

content.text: The engineer reported: "We are experiencing a major {{c1::infrastructure outage}} due to a {{c2::database bottleneck}}, and we are preparing a root cause analysis."
content.explanation: Outage means service downtime. **Bottleneck** refers to a point of congestion in a system that slows down performance. **Root cause analysis (RCA)** is the diagnostic process to find the source of the issue.
content.spanish: El ingeniero informó: "Estamos experimentando una interrupción importante de la infraestructura debido a un cuello de botella en la base de datos, y estamos preparando un análisis de causa raíz".
content.usage:
Pattern: experience an outage due to [cause] / conduct a root cause analysis
  • The network outage was caused by a configuration error.
  • We conducted a root cause analysis to prevent future failures.

content.text: Have you tried turning the device off and on again to {{c1::power cycle}} it?
content.explanation: To 'power cycle' a device means to shut off the power to a hardware piece and then turn it back on, which clears memory and resets state.
content.spanish: ¿Ha intentado apagar y volver a encender el dispositivo para reiniciar el ciclo de energía?
content.usage:
Pattern: power cycle / reboot the router
  • IT support always asks you to power cycle the modem first.
  • Power cycling the switch resolved the packet loss.

content.text: I will open a support ticket for this issue, and our team will {{c1::look into it}} shortly.
content.explanation: To 'look into' is a common phrasal verb meaning to investigate, examine, or check the status of a problem or request.
content.spanish: Abriré un ticket de soporte para este problema, y nuestro equipo lo revisará/investigará en breve.
content.usage:
Pattern: look into [issue / ticket / bug]
  • We are looking into the server latency report.
  • I'll look into why your payment was declined.

content.text: Please make sure your security credentials are up to date to prevent login {{c1::lockouts}}.
content.explanation: A 'lockout' is a security state where a user is temporarily or permanently barred from accessing an account after too many failed password attempts.
content.spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de que sus credenciales de seguridad estén actualizadas para evitar bloqueos de inicio de sesión.
content.usage:
Pattern: account lockout / lockout policy
  • Three failed attempts will trigger an automatic account lockout.
  • Contact IT support to resolve your password lockout.

content.text: We are currently experiencing a temporary server outage; we apologize for the {{c1::downtime}}.
content.explanation: 'Downtime' refers to the period during which a system, computer network, or server is unavailable or offline.
content.spanish: Actualmente estamos experimentando una interrupción temporal del servidor; nos disculpamos por el tiempo de inactividad.
content.usage:
Pattern: server downtime / system downtime
  • Scheduled maintenance downtime will occur this Sunday.
  • Our service level agreement guarantees less than 0.01% downtime.

content.text: Could you please send me a screenshot of the error message to help us {{c1::troubleshoot}} the problem?
content.explanation: To 'troubleshoot' means to identify, analyze, and resolve technical faults or bugs in a system by following a logical process of elimination.
content.spanish: ¿Podría por favor enviarme una captura de pantalla del mensaje de error para ayudarnos a diagnosticar/resolver el problema?
content.usage:
Pattern: troubleshoot the problem / technical troubleshooting
  • We need to troubleshoot why the API is returning 500 errors.
  • He is very good at troubleshooting network connection problems.

content.text: You need to update your operating system to the latest version to patch these {{c1::security vulnerabilities}}.
content.explanation: A 'security vulnerability' is a flaw or weakness in software or hardware that malicious actors can exploit to gain unauthorized access or execute code.
content.spanish: Necesita actualizar su sistema operativo a la última versión para parchear estas vulnerabilidades de seguridad.
content.usage:
Pattern: patch security vulnerabilities / zero-day vulnerability
  • The patch fixes critical security vulnerabilities in the kernel.
  • Cybercriminals scan the web looking for unpatched security vulnerabilities.

content.text: Please check your spam folder if you haven't received the password reset link {{c1::in your inbox}}.
content.explanation: An 'inbox' is the folder where newly received email messages are stored.
content.spanish: Por favor, revise su carpeta de correo no deseado (spam) si no ha recibido el enlace de restablecimiento de contraseña en su bandeja de entrada.
content.usage:
Pattern: in your inbox / email inbox
  • The confirmation code should arrive in your inbox in a minute.
  • My inbox is flooded with spam emails.

content.text: Could you please clear your browser cache and cookies to see if that resolves the display {{c1::glitch}}?
content.explanation: A 'glitch' is a minor, temporary, or sudden malfunction or technical fault in software, hardware, or a system.
content.spanish: ¿Podría por favor borrar la caché y las cookies de su navegador para ver si eso resuelve el fallo técnico de visualización?
content.usage:
Pattern: display glitch / software glitch
  • A temporary network glitch interrupted the database backup.
  • The developer patched the layout glitch in the landing page.

content.text: I will need to remote into your desktop to inspect the network configuration; do I have your {{c1::permission}}?
content.explanation: 'Permission' is the formal consent or authorization to do something, required in IT support before accessing client devices.
content.spanish: Necesitaré acceder de forma remota a su escritorio para inspeccionar la configuración de la red; ¿tengo su permiso?
content.usage:
Pattern: request permission / access permission
  • The agent asked for permission to install the updates.
  • You do not have permission to access this directory.

content.text: Please make sure your VPN connection is active before trying to access the internal database {{c1::servers}}.
content.explanation: A 'server' is a computer program or device that provides functionality or database storage for other programs or devices called clients.
content.spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de que su conexión VPN esté activa antes de intentar acceder a los servidores de la base de datos interna.
content.usage:
Pattern: database servers / cloud servers
  • The database servers are hosted in a secure VPC.
  • We migrated our production servers to Google Cloud.

content.text: We are upgrading our server infrastructure this weekend, so expect some temporary service {{c1::disruptions}}.
content.explanation: A 'disruption' is an event or activity that interrupts a process, system, or service from operating normally.
content.spanish: Estamos actualizando nuestra infraestructura de servidores este fin de semana, por lo que puede esperar algunas interrupciones temporales del servicio.
content.usage:
Pattern: service disruptions / temporary disruption
  • The storm caused major power disruptions in the city.
  • Minimize service disruptions by performing updates during off-peak hours.

content.text: Your ticket has been assigned to our network security specialist, and they will contact you to {{c1::resolve the issue}}.
content.explanation: To 'resolve the issue' is the standard formal collocation meaning to fix a technical problem, bug, or ticket successfully.
content.spanish: Su ticket ha sido asignado a nuestro especialista en seguridad de red, y se pondrá en contacto con usted para resolver el problema.
content.usage:
Pattern: resolve the issue / solve the problem
  • Our priority is to resolve the issue as quickly as possible.
  • IT support resolved the login issue in minutes.

content.text: Could you please verify that all cables are securely plugged into the docking station and the {{c1::monitor}}?
content.explanation: A 'monitor' is the computer screen that displays video and graphics output from a computer's processor.
content.spanish: ¿Podría por favor verificar que todos los cables estén bien conectados a la estación de acoplamiento y al monitor?
content.usage:
Pattern: computer monitor / dual-monitor setup
  • I set up a second monitor to improve my coding workflow.
  • The monitor went black because the power cord was loose.

content.text: We have reset your password; please use the temporary credentials to log in and create a new {{c1::password}}.
content.explanation: A 'password' is a secret word or string of characters used to verify identity and gain access to a computer system or account.
content.spanish: Hemos restablecido su contraseña; por favor use las credenciales temporales para iniciar sesión y crear una nueva contraseña.
content.usage:
Pattern: create a password / strong password
  • A strong password should include letters, numbers, and symbols.
  • I forgot my password and had to request a reset.

content.text: To prevent cyberattacks, all employees must complete the mandatory phishing awareness training by {{c1::Friday}}.
content.explanation: 'Friday' is the fifth day of the standard workweek, commonly set as a deadline for business training courses.
content.spanish: Para prevenir ataques cibernéticos, todos los empleados deben completar la capacitación obligatoria sobre concientización de phishing para el viernes.
content.usage:
Pattern: by Friday / Friday afternoon
  • Please submit the timesheet by Friday.
  • The office closes early on Friday.

content.text: Your computer has been flagged for using outdated software; please run the system updates {{c1::immediately}}.
content.explanation: 'Immediately' is an adverb meaning right away, without any delay or pause, highlighting urgency in security compliance.
content.spanish: Su computadora ha sido marcada por usar software desactualizado; por favor ejecute las actualizaciones del sistema de inmediato.
content.usage:
Pattern: immediately / without delay
  • If you see a security warning, contact IT support immediately.
  • The server was shut down immediately after the breach.

content.text: If the issue persists, please reply to this email thread or contact our IT support hotline directly at {{c1::extension 500}}.
content.explanation: A phone 'extension' is a secondary telephone line connected to a main switchboard in an office, identified by a short number.
content.spanish: Si el problema persiste, responda a este hilo de correo electrónico o póngase en contacto directamente con nuestra línea directa de soporte de TI en la extensión 500.
content.usage:
Pattern: extension [number] / direct extension
  • Dial the main office and enter extension 234.
  • What is your direct extension?

content.scenario: Meeting Efficiency: Parking Lot / Tabling Topics 🅿️
content.text: When a conversation goes off-track, you can say: "In the interest of time, let's {{c1::park this topic}} and {{c2::circle back to it}} at the end of the meeting."
content.explanation: In the interest of time means to save time. **Park a topic** (or put it in the 'parking lot') means to temporarily postpone discussing it. **Circle back** means to return to a topic later.
content.spanish: Cuando una conversación se desvía, puedes decir: "Por razones de tiempo, guardemos este tema en el tintero y volvamos a él al final de la reunión".
content.usage:
Pattern: In the interest of time, let's park [topic] and circle back to it [time]
  • In the interest of time, let's park the budget discussion and circle back to it on Friday.
  • I want to circle back to what you said about API keys.

content.text: If a detailed discussion only concerns a few people: "This seems to require a deeper dive, so let's {{c1::take this offline}} and let the rest of the team {{c2::get some time back}}."
content.explanation: Take this offline is an idiom meaning to discuss a topic privately outside of the current meeting. **Get some time back** is a polite way to end a meeting early or release people who are not needed.
content.spanish: Si una discusión detallada solo concierne a unas pocas personas: "Esto parece requerir un análisis más profundo, así que hablemos de esto fuera de línea y dejemos que el resto del equipo recupere algo de tiempo".
content.usage:
Pattern: take this offline / get [amount of] time back
  • Let's take the database schema discussion offline.
  • If we finish early, everyone gets 10 minutes back.

content.text: Let's {{c1::keep this discussion high-level}} today, as we only have 15 minutes left on the agenda.
content.explanation: Keep high-level means to focus on the big picture, summaries, and general strategies rather than getting caught up in minor details or technicalities.
content.spanish: Para evitar empantanarse en los detalles: "Mantengamos esta discusión a un nivel general hoy, ya que solo nos quedan 15 minutos en la agenda".
content.usage:
Pattern: keep this [discussion/sync] high-level
  • Let's keep this sync high-level and discuss implementation next week.
  • She kept the presentation high-level for the executives.

content.text: I want to make sure we {{c1::stick to the agenda}} so we can {{c2::cover all the items}} we planned for today.
content.explanation: Stick to the agenda means to follow the pre-planned list of topics. **Cover items** means to discuss and resolve the topics.
content.spanish: Quiero asegurarme de que nos apeguemos a la agenda para que podamos cubrir todos los temas que planeamos para hoy.
content.usage:
Pattern: stick to the agenda / cover the items
  • If we stick to the agenda, we will finish on time.
  • We have a lot of items to cover in today's standup.

content.text: Let's keep this meeting productive by stick to the agenda and avoiding {{c1::going off on tangents}}.
content.explanation: To 'go off on a tangent' means to suddenly start talking about a subject that is not directly related to the main topic of conversation.
content.spanish: Mantengamos esta reunión productiva apegándonos a la agenda y evitando irnos por las ramas/desviarnos del tema.
content.usage:
Pattern: go off on a tangent / deviate from agenda
  • We wasted fifteen minutes because the speaker went off on a tangent.
  • Let's focus on the bug and avoid going off on tangents.

content.text: We only have thirty minutes, so let's {{c1::get straight to the point}}.
content.explanation: To 'get straight to the point' means to address the most important matter directly, without wasting time on introductory or irrelevant remarks.
content.spanish: Solo tenemos treinta minutos, así que vayamos directo al grano.
content.usage:
Pattern: get straight to the point / cut to the chase
  • I'll get straight to the point: our server was hacked.
  • Baristas appreciate it when customers get straight to the point.

content.text: Could you please send out the meeting minutes and list the {{c1::action items}} by end of day?
content.explanation: An 'action item' is a specific task, chore, or action identified during a meeting that must be executed by a designated team member.
content.spanish: ¿Podrías por favor enviar la minuta de la reunión y listar las tareas pendientes/puntos de acción para el final del día?
content.usage:
Pattern: action items / list of tasks
  • We assigned the action items to the developers.
  • Please review your action items before the next sync.

content.text: Let's schedule a brief stand-up meeting tomorrow to check in on our progress and {{c1::realign}}.
content.explanation: To 'realign' means to adjust or coordinate resources, strategies, or team members back into correct alignment or agreement regarding goals.
content.spanish: Agendemos una breve reunión diaria (stand-up) mañana para revisar nuestro progreso y volver a alinearnos.
content.usage:
Pattern: realign resources / realign expectations
  • We need to realign our strategy to match the new budget.
  • The team met to realign after the project scope changed.

content.text: We need to respect everyone's time and make sure we start and end our meetings {{c1::on schedule}}.
content.explanation: 'On schedule' (or on time) means at the planned or expected time, according to the timetable or agenda.
content.spanish: Necesitamos respetar el tiempo de todos y asegurarnos de comenzar y terminar nuestras reuniones a tiempo/según lo programado.
content.usage:
Pattern: on schedule / behind schedule / ahead of schedule
  • The train arrived exactly on schedule.
  • We must complete the deployment on schedule.

content.text: Please review the prep materials before the meeting so we can focus on making {{c1::key decisions}}.
content.explanation: A 'key decision' is a highly important or critical resolution that determines the path, budget, or success of a project or firm.
content.spanish: Por favor, revise los materiales de preparación antes de la reunión para que podamos enfocarnos en tomar decisiones clave.
content.usage:
Pattern: make key decisions / decisive actions
  • The board met to make key decisions regarding the merger.
  • We need senior management to make key decisions on the architecture.

content.text: Let's make sure we stick to the time limits for each agenda item to avoid running {{c1::over time}}.
content.explanation: To 'run over time' (or run late) means that a meeting or task takes longer than the scheduled duration, disrupting subsequent events.
content.spanish: Asegurémonos de apegarnos a los límites de tiempo para cada punto de la agenda para evitar pasarnos del tiempo.
content.usage:
Pattern: run over time / run late
  • The presentation ran over time, so we had to skip the Q&A.
  • Please notify the client if you are running late.

content.text: We only have ten minutes left, so let's skip the details and focus on the main {{c1::takeaway}}.
content.explanation: A 'takeaway' (or key learning) is a primary fact, conclusion, or piece of information that you learn or retain from a discussion or meeting.
content.spanish: Solo nos quedan diez minutos, así que omitamos los detalles y enfoquémonos en la conclusión principal/punto clave.
content.usage:
Pattern: main takeaway / key takeaway
  • What is the main takeaway from the post-mortem report?
  • My key takeaway was that we must run stress tests before launching.

content.text: Could you please share the slide deck before the call so everyone can review the data in {{c1::advance}}?
content.explanation: To do something 'in advance' (or beforehand) means doing it ahead of time, before a specific event or deadline.
content.spanish: ¿Podrías por favor compartir la presentación de diapositivas antes de la llamada para que todos puedan revisar los datos con anticipación?
content.usage:
Pattern: in advance / beforehand
  • Please submit your requests two days in advance.
  • I booked the train tickets in advance to save money.

content.text: Let's schedule a follow-up meeting next week to review progress on our action {{c1::items}}.
content.explanation: 'Action items' are the specific tasks assigned to individuals during a meeting, here referenced in the plural form.
content.spanish: Agendemos una reunión de seguimiento la próxima semana para revisar el progreso de nuestras tareas pendientes.
content.usage:
Pattern: action items / list of tasks
  • We documented all action items in the shared document.
  • Let's make sure we execute our action items on schedule.

content.text: We need to respect the meeting host's agenda and avoid introducing unrelated topics without prior {{c1::notice}}.
content.explanation: 'Prior notice' (or advance warning) is a notification given ahead of time regarding a change, request, or event.
content.spanish: Necesitamos respetar la agenda del anfitrión de la reunión y evitar introducir temas no relacionados sin previo aviso.
content.usage:
Pattern: prior notice / short notice
  • You must give two weeks' prior notice before taking vacation.
  • Thank you for attending on such short notice.

content.text: Please make sure your microphone is muted when you are not speaking to minimize background {{c1::noise}}.
content.explanation: 'Background noise' is any extraneous, unwanted sound (like traffic, construction, or typing) that interferes with communication during a call.
content.spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de silenciar su micrófono cuando no esté hablando para minimizar el ruido de fondo.
content.usage:
Pattern: background noise / extraneous sound
  • There was too much background noise during the call.
  • Use a headset to reduce background noise.

content.text: If you have any detailed feedback, please send it via email to avoid holding up the rest of the {{c1::group}}.
content.explanation: To 'hold up' the group is a phrasal verb meaning to delay, block, or slow down progress for everyone in the meeting.
content.spanish: Si tiene algún comentario detallado, envíelo por correo electrónico para evitar retrasar al resto del grupo.
content.usage:
Pattern: hold up the process / delay the team
  • We don't want to hold up the release, so deploy the hotfix now.
  • The accident held up traffic for hours.

content.text: Let's take this discussion offline and schedule a separate call with the database engineers to {{c1::discuss the details}}.
content.explanation: To 'discuss the details' is the standard collocation meaning to evaluate the specifics, technical details, or fine points of a plan.
content.spanish: Llevemos esta discusión fuera de la reunión (offline) y programemos una llamada separada con los ingenieros de bases de datos para discutir los detalles.
content.usage:
Pattern: discuss the details / evaluate specifics
  • We met to discuss the details of the API integration.
  • The post-mortem report discusses the details of the crash.

content.text: We have reached our goals for this meeting, so let's adjourn and get back to our daily {{c1::tasks}}.
