content.explanation: 'Daily tasks' (or daily chores/duties) are the routine activities that an employee must execute each day in their job.
content.spanish: Hemos logrado nuestros objetivos para esta reunión, así que levantemos la sesión y volvamos a nuestras tareas diarias.
content.usage:
Pattern: daily tasks / routine workload
  • Writing unit tests is one of my daily tasks.
  • He organized his daily tasks on a Kanban board.

content.text: Thank you all for joining today; I will share the recording of this session with the team members who were {{c1::absent}}.
content.explanation: 'Absent' is an adjective meaning not present in a place, meeting, or event.
content.spanish: Gracias a todos por unirse hoy; compartiré la grabación de esta sesión con los miembros del equipo que estuvieron ausentes.
content.usage:
Pattern: absent from work / absent members
  • He was absent from the meeting because of illness.
  • The secretary noted the names of the absent board members.

content.text: We cannot agree to these terms unless you include a {{c1::termination clause}} for convenience.
content.explanation: A termination clause is a contract provision that defines the conditions under which the agreement can be ended. For convenience means it can be terminated without needing a specific cause or breach.
content.spanish: No podemos aceptar estos términos a menos que incluya una cláusula de rescisión por conveniencia.
content.usage:
Pattern: termination clause / terminate for convenience
  • The contract contains a 30-day termination clause.
  • Either party may terminate the agreement for convenience with written notice.

content.text: During the review, he negotiated: "Based on the {{c1::market rate}} and my contributions, I'd like to adjust my {{c2::compensation package}} to include remote-work fringe benefits."
content.explanation: Market rate is the standard salary for a role. **Compensation package** includes base salary, bonuses, and benefits. **Fringe benefits** are extra benefits (like gym memberships or health insurance).
content.spanish: Durante la revisión, negoció: "Basándome en la tarifa del mercado y mis contribuciones, me gustaría ajustar mi paquete de compensación para incluir beneficios adicionales de trabajo remoto".
content.usage:
Pattern: negotiate a compensation package based on market rate / include fringe benefits
  • Our company offers an attractive compensation package with great fringe benefits.
  • The offer is below the current market rate for senior developers.

content.text: We are willing to make concessions, but we need you to match us on the {{c1::payment terms}}.
content.explanation: 'Payment terms' are the conditions under which a seller will complete a sale, specifying when payment is due and any discounts or fees (e.g. Net 30).
content.spanish: Estamos dispuestos a hacer concesiones, pero necesitamos que esté de acuerdo con nosotros en las condiciones de pago.
content.usage:
Pattern: payment terms / Net 30 / Net 60
  • The contract specifies payment terms of Net 45.
  • We negotiated better payment terms with our main vendor.

content.text: Our goal is to reach a win-win agreement that benefits both parties in the {{c1::long run}}.
content.explanation: The 'long run' is an idiomatic expression referring to a long period of time in the future, as opposed to the immediate present.
content.spanish: Nuestra meta es llegar a un acuerdo de beneficio mutuo que beneficie a ambas partes a largo plazo.
content.usage:
Pattern: in the long run / long-term
  • This software is expensive, but it will save us money in the long run.
  • Our decisions must benefit the project in the long run.

content.text: We cannot accept these terms; they do not align with our risk management {{c1::guidelines}}.
content.explanation: 'Guidelines' are official rules, principles, or instructions that dictate how a task or business policy must be conducted.
content.spanish: No podemos aceptar estas condiciones; no se alinean con nuestras directrices/pautas de gestión de riesgos.
content.usage:
Pattern: guidelines / company policy
  • Please review the branding guidelines before making the logo.
  • Our risk management guidelines prohibit this setup.

content.text: Let's discuss the pricing structure: is there any room for a discount on {{c1::bulk orders}}?
content.explanation: A 'bulk order' (or bulk purchase) is an order for a very large quantity of a product, which typically qualifies for a discount.
content.spanish: Hablemos de la estructura de precios: ¿hay algún margen para un descuento en pedidos al por mayor?
content.usage:
Pattern: bulk order / bulk discount
  • We save 20% on paper supplies by placing a bulk order.
  • The supplier offers a discount for bulk orders.

content.text: We value our partnership and want to find a solution that doesn't {{c1::compromise quality}}.
content.explanation: To 'compromise quality' means to accept a lower standard or degrade the quality of a product or service, often to save money or time.
content.spanish: Valoramos nuestra asociación y queremos encontrar una solución que no comprometa la calidad.
content.usage:
Pattern: compromise quality / lower standards
  • We want to reduce costs, but we will never compromise quality.
  • Cutting budget now might compromise the quality of the release.

content.text: Could we extend the contract duration in exchange for a lower annual service {{c1::fee}}?
content.explanation: A 'service fee' is a charge made by a business for performing work or services for a client (distinct from product cost).
content.spanish: ¿Podríamos extender la duración del contrato a cambio de una tarifa/cuota de servicio anual más baja?
content.usage:
Pattern: service fee / annual fee
  • The contract includes an annual maintenance service fee.
  • The bank charges a service fee for international transfers.

content.text: Before we sign the agreement, we need to consult with our legal counsel to {{c1::review the clauses}}.
content.explanation: To 'review the clauses' means to read and evaluate the specific sections, paragraphs, or provisions of a legal contract to ensure they are acceptable.
content.spanish: Antes de firmar el acuerdo, necesitamos consultar con nuestro asesor legal para revisar las cláusulas.
content.usage:
Pattern: review the clauses / contract clauses
  • Our legal team needs to review the liability clauses.
  • We requested changes to the termination clauses.

content.text: We have reached an agreement on the scope, so let's move forward and {{c1::finalize the contract}}.
content.explanation: To 'finalize the contract' means to complete the last details of a legal agreement and prepare it for signing by both parties.
content.spanish: Hemos llegado a un acuerdo sobre el alcance, así que sigamos adelante y finalicemos el contrato.
content.usage:
Pattern: finalize the contract / sign contract
  • We are meeting tomorrow to finalize the contract.
  • Once we finalize the contract, we can begin production.

content.text: We are open to negotiating the contract price if you can commit to a multi-year {{c1::service agreement}}.
content.explanation: A 'service agreement' (or service contract) is a legal document defining the terms, pricing, and support details for services provided by a vendor.
content.spanish: Estamos abiertos a negociar el precio del contrato si puede comprometerse con un acuerdo de servicio plurianual.
content.usage:
Pattern: service agreement / SLA contract
  • We signed a three-year service agreement with the cloud provider.
  • The service agreement guarantees next-day hardware replacement.

content.text: Our objective is to build a long-term partnership based on mutual trust and shared {{c1::goals}}.
content.explanation: 'Shared goals' are objectives or aims that are held in common by two partnering companies or departments, facilitating collaboration.
content.spanish: Nuestro objetivo es construir una asociación a largo plazo basada en la confianza mutua y los objetivos compartidos.
content.usage:
Pattern: shared goals / common objectives
  • Partnerships succeed when both companies have shared goals.
  • Let's define our shared goals before launching the joint venture.

content.text: We cannot agree to these terms; they place too much financial risk on our {{c1::company}}.
content.explanation: A 'company' (or corporation) is a commercial business or legal entity formed by a group of people to engage in trade.
content.spanish: No podemos aceptar estas condiciones; representan demasiado riesgo financiero para nuestra empresa.
content.usage:
Pattern: our company / corporate entity
  • Our company is expanding its operations in Europe.
  • The merger consolidated the two companies into one.

content.text: Let's discuss the delivery timeline: can you guarantee delivery within thirty days of signing the {{c1::contract}}?
content.explanation: A 'contract' is a written or spoken agreement, especially one concerning employment, sales, or tenancy, that is intended to be enforceable by law.
content.spanish: Hablemos del cronograma de entrega: ¿puede garantizar la entrega dentro de los treinta días posteriores a la firma del contrato?
content.usage:
Pattern: sign the contract / draft a contract
  • Make sure you read the fine print before you sign the contract.
  • We signed the contract yesterday.

content.text: We understand your constraints, but we must protect our profit margins to sustain our operations {{c1::long term}}.
content.explanation: 'Long term' refers to a long period of time in the future, especially regarding business planning or financial returns.
content.spanish: Entendemos sus limitaciones, pero debemos proteger nuestros márgenes de beneficio para sostener nuestras operaciones a largo plazo.
content.usage:
Pattern: long term / short term
  • Our strategy focuses on long-term sustainability rather than quick profits.
  • This setup is not viable long term.

content.text: Could we reduce the payment frequency in exchange for a slightly lower interest {{c1::rate}}?
content.explanation: An 'interest rate' is the percentage of a loan amount charged by a lender to a borrower for the use of assets.
content.spanish: ¿Podríamos reducir la frecuencia de pago a cambio de una tasa de interés ligeramente más baja?
content.usage:
Pattern: interest rate / annual interest rate
  • The bank increased its prime interest rate.
  • We negotiated a fixed interest rate for the business loan.

content.text: Before we sign the contract, we need to verify that all clauses are compliant with local {{c1::regulations}}.
content.explanation: 'Regulations' are rules or directives made and maintained by an authority (like a government agency) governing business practices.
content.spanish: Antes de firmar el contrato, necesitamos verificar que todas las cláusulas cumplan con las regulaciones locales.
content.usage:
Pattern: compliance regulations / government regulations
  • Our data servers comply with all European regulations (GDPR).
  • Failing to follow environmental regulations leads to heavy fines.

content.text: We have reached an agreement on all key points; let's draft the final contract and sign it {{c1::tomorrow}}.
content.explanation: 'Tomorrow' is the day after today, commonly set as a target date in contract negotiations once terms are aligned.
content.spanish: Hemos llegado a un acuerdo en todos los puntos clave; redactemos el contrato final y firmémoslo mañana.
content.usage:
Pattern: sign it tomorrow / schedule for tomorrow
  • The documents will be ready for signing tomorrow morning.
  • Let's meet tomorrow to review the feedback.

content.text: We are willing to compromise on the delivery method if you can cover the shipping {{c1::costs}}.
content.explanation: 'Shipping costs' are the expenses required to package and transport physical goods from the seller to the buyer.
content.spanish: Estamos dispuestos a ceder en el método de entrega si usted puede cubrir los costos de envío.
content.usage:
Pattern: shipping costs / freight charges
  • The online price does not include shipping costs.
  • We offer free shipping costs for orders over $500.

content.text: Let's review the termination clause: what is the notice period required to end the contract without {{c1::penalties}}?
content.explanation: A 'penalty' is a punishment or financial fine imposed by contract terms for failing to comply with a clause (such as early termination).
content.spanish: Revisemos la cláusula de rescisión: ¿cuál es el período de notificación requerido para finalizar el contrato sin penalizaciones?
content.usage:
Pattern: contract penalties / cancellation fee
  • The contract includes financial penalties for late delivery.
  • You can cancel the service after one year without penalties.

content.text: Let's {{c1::touch base}} tomorrow to discuss the updates.
content.explanation: touch base is a common business idiom meaning to briefly contact or meet with someone to talk about progress.
content.spanish: Póngamonos en contacto mañana para discutir las actualizaciones.
content.usage:
Pattern: touch base with [someone] [time]
  • I need to touch base with my manager before the client call.
  • Let's touch base next week.

content.text: We need to {{c1::push back}} the meeting to 3 PM.
content.explanation: push back means to postpone or reschedule an event to a later time or date.
content.spanish: Necesitamos posponer la reunión para las 3 PM.
content.usage:
Pattern: push back [event] to [time/date]
  • Can we push back the project deadline by two days?
  • They had to push back the launch due to technical issues.

content.text: I'll {{c1::follow up with}} you via email once I get the data.
content.explanation: follow up with means to contact someone again to provide further information, verify progress, or request updates.
content.spanish: Le daré seguimiento por correo electrónico una vez que obtenga los datos.
content.usage:
Pattern: follow up with [someone] on/via [method]
  • The doctor will follow up with you next month.
  • I will follow up with the client on the contract details tomorrow.

content.text: During the planning sync, the director shared: "Our {{c1::financial forecast}} projects new {{c2::revenue streams}} from our enterprise API licensing model."
content.explanation: Financial forecast is a projection of future financial performance. **Revenue streams** refer to different sources of income for a business.
content.spanish: Durante la sincronización de planificación, el director compartió: "Nuestro pronóstico financiero proyecta nuevas fuentes de ingresos a partir de nuestro modelo de licencia de API empresarial".
content.usage:
Pattern: financial forecast projects [metric] / identify new revenue streams
  • The financial forecast projects a 15% increase in sales.
  • We are exploring new revenue streams to diversify our income.

content.text: Could you please take notes during the presentation and share them with the team {{c1::afterwards}}?
content.explanation: 'Afterwards' is an adverb meaning at a later or subsequent time; after something else has occurred.
content.spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar notas durante la presentación y compartirlas con el equipo después/posteriormente?
content.usage:
Pattern: afterwards / after the meeting
  • We had a brief discussion afterwards.
  • She completed the task and left immediately afterwards.

content.text: Let's go around the table and have everyone briefly introduce themselves and their {{c1::area of expertise}}.
content.explanation: An 'area of expertise' is the specific field, subject, or skill set in which a person is highly knowledgeable or skilled.
content.spanish: Hagamos una ronda en la mesa para que todos se presenten brevemente y mencionen su área de especialización.
content.usage:
Pattern: area of expertise / domain knowledge
  • Database performance tuning is my main area of expertise.
  • We hired consultants with expertise in cloud migration.

content.text: I'd like to share my screen to show you the latest mockup designs for the {{c1::landing page}}.
content.explanation: A 'landing page' is the specific web page that a user arrives at after clicking a link or advertisement, designed to convert visitors.
content.spanish: Me gustaría compartir mi pantalla para mostrarles los últimos diseños de maqueta para la página de destino (inicio).
content.usage:
Pattern: landing page / homepage
  • We optimized the landing page to increase sign-ups.
  • The marketing campaign directs traffic to a custom landing page.

content.text: Let's reserve the last ten minutes of the meeting for a quick Q&A session to address any {{c1::remaining questions}}.
content.explanation: A 'Q&A session' (Question and Answer) is a dedicated time during a meeting or presentation for the audience to ask questions of the speaker.
content.spanish: Reservemos los últimos diez minutos de la reunión para una sesión rápida de preguntas y respuestas para abordar cualquier pregunta restante.
content.usage:
Pattern: Q&A session / questions and answers
  • The webinar ended with a lively Q&A session.
  • We will take questions during the Q&A session at the end.

content.text: I agree with your suggestion, let's incorporate that feedback into the next iteration of the {{c1::proposal}}.
content.explanation: A 'proposal' is a formal written document or offer presented to others for consideration, typically detailing a plan, budget, or contract.
content.spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su sugerencia, incorporemos esa retroalimentación en la próxima iteración de la propuesta.
content.usage:
Pattern: project proposal / business proposal
  • We submitted our proposal to the client yesterday.
  • The board will review the proposal at the next meeting.

content.text: Before we begin, let's quickly review the {{c1::agenda items}} to make sure we allocate our time properly.
content.explanation: Are the individual topics or tasks scheduled to be discussed during a meeting
content.spanish: Antes de comenzar, revisemos rápidamente los puntos de la agenda para asegurarnos de asignar nuestro tiempo adecuadamente.
content.usage:
Pattern: agenda items / meeting agenda
  • We have five agenda items to cover today.
  • Please add this topic to the agenda items for next week.

content.text: Could you please take minutes during the meeting and email them to the attendees {{c1::by tomorrow morning}}?
content.explanation: 'By tomorrow morning' is a common business deadline, requesting that a task be completed and sent before the start of the next workday.
content.spanish: ¿Podrías por favor tomar la minuta durante la reunión y enviarla por correo electrónico a los asistentes para mañana por la mañana?
content.usage:
Pattern: by tomorrow morning / first thing tomorrow
  • I'll have the code review ready by tomorrow morning.
  • Please send the contract to the client by tomorrow morning.

content.text: Let's introduce our new team member, Colin, who will be taking over the database migration {{c1::project}}.
content.explanation: A 'project' is an individual or collaborative enterprise that is carefully planned and designed to achieve a particular aim.
content.spanish: Presentemos a nuestro nuevo miembro del equipo, Colin, quien se hará cargo del proyecto de migración de la base de datos.
content.usage:
Pattern: project manager / project milestones
  • I am managing the new API development project.
  • The database migration project was completed on schedule.

content.text: I'd like to present the user flow diagram to show how the customer registers on our {{c1::platform}}.
content.explanation: A 'platform' is a major software environment, website, or infrastructure where services are hosted and executed.
content.spanish: Me gustaría presentar el diagrama de flujo de usuario para mostrar cómo el cliente se registra en nuestra plataforma.
content.usage:
Pattern: software platform / cloud platform
  • Our platform supports over 10,000 concurrent connections.
  • We deployed our application on a secure hosting platform.

content.text: We will allocate the last fifteen minutes of the meeting for an open discussion to address any {{c1::concerns}}.
content.explanation: A 'concern' is a matter of interest, anxiety, or worry raised by team members or clients regarding a plan or change.
content.spanish: Asignaremos los últimos quince minutos de la reunión para una discusión abierta para abordar cualquier preocupación.
content.usage:
Pattern: address concerns / voice concerns
  • The director met with staff to address their concerns about the restructuring.
  • If you have any safety concerns, please voice them immediately.

content.text: I agree with your proposal; let's schedule a follow-up call to plan the implementation {{c1::steps}}.
content.explanation: 'Steps' refer to the phases or individual tasks required to carry out a proposal or plan.
content.spanish: Estoy de acuerdo con su propuesta; programemos una llamada de seguimiento para planificar los pasos de implementación.
content.usage:
Pattern: implementation steps / action steps
  • The document outlines the implementation steps for the database migration.
  • We followed the configuration steps exactly.

content.text: Please make sure you have the report ready before the meeting starts so we can reference the {{c1::metrics}}.
content.explanation: 'Metrics' are measures of quantitative assessment commonly used for assessing, comparing, and tracking performance (e.g. latency, conversions).
content.spanish: Por favor, asegúrese de tener el informe listo antes de que comience la reunión para que podamos hacer referencia a las métricas.
content.usage:
Pattern: performance metrics / tracking metrics
  • Our primary performance metric is API response time.
  • Review the marketing metrics before launching the campaign.

content.text: If there are no objections, let's proceed with the plan and assign tasks to the respective team {{c1::members}}.
content.explanation: A 'member' is an individual belonging to a group or team.
content.spanish: Si no hay objeciones, procedamos con el plan y asignemos tareas a los respectivos miembros del equipo.
content.usage:
Pattern: team members / staff members
  • All team members must attend the daily stand-up meeting.
  • We welcomed three new members to our division.

content.text: I'd like to share a quick update on our customer acquisition costs for this past {{c1::quarter}}.
content.explanation: A 'quarter' (Q1, Q2, Q3, Q4) is a three-month period in a financial calendar, used to report business earnings and metrics.
content.spanish: Me gustaría compartir una actualización rápida sobre nuestros costos de adquisición de clientes para este último trimestre.
content.usage:
Pattern: fiscal quarter / third quarter / Q3
  • Our revenue increased by 10% in the last quarter.
  • We are planning our goals for the upcoming quarter.

content.text: Let's keep the discussion focused on the project milestones and avoid discussing future product {{c1::features}}.
content.explanation: A 'feature' is a distinctive attribute or aspect of a software application (e.g. user authentication, dark mode).
content.spanish: Mantengamos la discusión enfocada en los hitos del proyecto y evitemos discutir futuras características del producto.
content.usage:
Pattern: product features / new features
  • The developer is coding the new search feature.
  • Users requested a data export feature.

content.text: Thank you all for your feedback today; we will use these insights to refine the project design before our next {{c1::sync}}.
content.explanation: A 'sync' (synchronization) is business jargon for a regular meeting scheduled to align team members on project updates.
content.spanish: Gracias a todos por sus comentarios de hoy; utilizaremos estos conocimientos para refinar el diseño del proyecto antes de nuestra próxima reunión de sincronización.
content.usage:
Pattern: weekly sync / schedule a sync
  • Let's schedule a quick sync call tomorrow morning.
  • The weekly sync is scheduled for Tuesdays at 10:00 AM.

content.text: The 5 Whys technique involves asking '{{c1::Why?}}' repeatedly until you reach the root cause, typically 3-5 levels deep — not just the proximate cause.
content.explanation: The 5 Whys was developed by Taiichi Ohno at Toyota. The power is stopping at the systemic root, not the first obvious answer. Example: Why did the service fail? → Config was wrong. Why was config wrong? → Deploy script had a bug. Why did that bug reach prod? → Code review skipped due to time pressure. Why? → Deadline pressure from leadership. Root cause: organizational incentive structure.
content.spanish: La técnica de los 5 Por qués consiste en preguntar '¿Por qué?' repetidamente hasta llegar a la causa raíz, típicamente 3-5 niveles de profundidad — no solo la causa próxima. Desarrollada por Toyota.
content.usage: • Why #1: "The deployment failed." → Why #2: "The config file had incorrect values." → Why #3: "The template was outdated." → Why #4: "We had no automated config validation." → Why #5: "Config validation wasn't part of our CI/CD pipeline." Root cause: missing CI gate.

content.scenario: RCA Language 🔍: Proximate vs. Root Cause
content.text: The proximate cause is {{c1::the immediate trigger of the incident}}, while the root cause is {{c1::the underlying systemic condition that made the trigger possible}}.
content.explanation: Fixing only the proximate cause is like treating symptoms, not disease. Example: Proximate — a developer pushed bad code to production. Root cause — there was no automated test gate blocking bad code from reaching prod. Fixing the proximate (fire the developer) doesn't fix the systemic gap; fixing the root (add the CI gate) prevents recurrence for all developers.
content.spanish: La causa próxima es el desencadenante inmediato del incidente. La causa raíz es la condición sistémica subyacente que hizo posible el desencadenante. Arreglar solo la causa próxima es tratar síntomas, no la enfermedad.
content.usage:
• Proximate: "The developer deployed on a Friday afternoon without testing."
  • Root cause: "The deployment policy didn't block Friday afternoon pushes and the CI pipeline lacked mandatory smoke tests."
  • Always ask: "If a different person did the same thing, would this happen again?" If yes, you haven't reached the root cause.

content.text: A standard postmortem document contains: {{c1::Timeline, Impact, Root Cause, Contributing Factors, Action Items with owners and dates, and Lessons Learned}}.
content.explanation: Google SRE defines postmortem as a written record that ensures an incident is documented, its root cause is understood, and preventive actions are implemented. The timeline is factual (no blame). Contributing factors are conditions that made the root cause more likely. Action items must be SMART: specific, measurable, assigned, realistic, time-bound.
content.spanish: Un postmortem estándar contiene: Cronología, Impacto, Causa Raíz, Factores Contribuyentes, Elementos de Acción con dueños y fechas, y Lecciones Aprendidas. Según el estándar Google SRE.
content.usage:
• Template sections:
    1. Summary (2-3 sentences)
    2. Timeline (bullet points, UTC timestamps)
    3. Impact (users, duration, revenue)
    4. Root cause
    5. Contributing factors
    6. Action items (who + what + by when)
    7. Lessons learned

content.scenario: RCA Language 🔍: Action Item Language in RCAs
content.text: Weak RCA action items say 'improve monitoring'. Strong ones say: "{{c1::Add a PagerDuty alert for error rate > 5% on the auth service, owned by [name], by [specific date].}}"
content.explanation: Vague action items in postmortems never get done. 'Improve monitoring', 'strengthen processes', 'better communication' — these are aspirations, not actions. Every action item needs: a specific change, an owner (one person), and a deadline. The test: can you verify in 30 seconds whether this was done?
content.spanish: Elementos de acción débiles dicen 'mejorar el monitoreo'. Los fuertes dicen: 'Agregar una alerta de PagerDuty para tasa de error > 5% en el servicio de auth, a cargo de [nombre], antes del [fecha específica].' Cada acción necesita: un cambio específico, un dueño y una fecha límite.
content.usage:
• Weak: "We'll improve our deployment process."
  • Strong: "Add a mandatory pre-prod smoke test to the CI pipeline, owned by DevOps lead, completed by 2026-07-28."
  • Weak: "Better alerting."
  • Strong: "Configure PagerDuty p50 latency alert at 500ms threshold for payment-api, owned by SRE team, by July 21."

content.scenario: RCA Language 🔍: Contributing Factors vs. Root Cause
content.text: Contributing factors are {{c1::conditions that increased the likelihood or severity of an incident without being the direct cause}} — for example, time pressure, unclear ownership, or lack of documentation.
content.explanation: Modern incident analysis (following Sidney Dekker's Safety Differently approach) recognizes that most incidents have multiple contributing factors alongside a root cause. Identifying contributing factors helps address the organizational conditions that make incidents more likely, not just the specific technical failure.
content.spanish: Los factores contribuyentes son condiciones que aumentaron la probabilidad o gravedad de un incidente sin ser la causa directa — por ejemplo, presión de tiempo, propiedad poco clara o falta de documentación.
content.usage:
• Root cause: "No rate limiting on the API endpoint."
  • Contributing factors: "(1) The team was under deadline pressure and skipped the security review. (2) There was no checklist item for rate limiting in the deploy process. (3) Oncall rotation had a gap that delayed initial detection."

content.text: When presenting RCA findings to customers or leadership, use: "{{c1::We identified the root cause as X. We've taken Y action to prevent recurrence, and here's what we're monitoring to confirm effectiveness.}}"
content.explanation: This three-part customer RCA communication (cause → fix → validation) gives stakeholders the three things they most need: understanding, confidence, and assurance. Skipping the validation part ('what we're watching') leaves customers wondering if the fix will actually hold.
content.spanish: 'Identificamos la causa raíz como X. Hemos tomado la acción Y para prevenir la recurrencia, y esto es lo que estamos monitoreando para confirmar la efectividad.' — comunicación de RCA al cliente: causa + corrección + validación.
content.usage: • "We identified that the root cause was an unvalidated configuration change in our load balancer rules. We've reverted the change, added a mandatory validation step to our deployment pipeline, and we're monitoring error rates on a 5-minute interval for the next 72 hours."

content.text: Let's not {{c1::get bogged down in}} the implementation details during this high-level sync.
content.explanation: get bogged down in means to become so stuck or overwhelmed by small details that you lose sight of the main goal and stop making progress.
content.spanish: No nos empantanemos en los detalles de implementación durante esta sincronización de alto nivel.
content.usage:
Pattern: get bogged down in [details/logistics/work]
  • Don't get bogged down in minor edits; focus on completing the draft.
  • Our discussion got bogged down in budget arguments.

content.text: Welcome everyone; let's start by reviewing the agenda for today's call and identifying our {{c1::key objectives}}.
content.explanation: 'Key objectives' are the primary, most important goals or outcomes that a meeting or project is designed to achieve.
content.spanish: Bienvenidos todos; comencemos revisando la agenda para la llamada de hoy e identificando nuestros objetivos clave.
content.usage:
Pattern: key objectives / main goals
  • The key objectives of this phase are database cleanup and indexing.
  • What are the key objectives for today's meeting?

content.text: I'll be facilitating the discussion today, and I want to make sure everyone has an opportunity to {{c1::voice their opinion}}.
content.explanation: To 'voice your opinion' means to express your thoughts, beliefs, or views on a subject openly and publicly.
content.spanish: Estaré moderando la discusión hoy, y quiero asegurarme de que todos tengan la oportunidad de expresar su opinión.
content.usage:
Pattern: voice opinion / express views
  • Employees are encouraged to voice their opinions in team surveys.
  • He was not afraid to voice his opinion during the board meeting.

content.text: Let's keep an eye on the time: we need to move on to the next topic on the agenda in {{c1::five minutes}}.
content.explanation: To 'keep an eye on the time' means to monitor the clock continuously to ensure the meeting doesn't run late.
content.spanish: Estemos atentos al tiempo: necesitamos pasar al siguiente tema de la agenda en cinco minutos.
content.usage:
Pattern: keep an eye on the time / track time
  • We must keep an eye on the time to avoid missing our flight.
  • I am keeping an eye on the time so we finish before noon.

content.text: Could you please keep your updates concise so we have enough time to discuss the new {{c1::marketing campaign}}?
content.explanation: A 'marketing campaign' is an organized, strategic course of action to promote a product, service, or brand across media channels.
content.spanish: ¿Podría por favor mantener sus actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir la nueva campaña de marketing?
content.usage:
Pattern: marketing campaign / advertising campaign
  • We are launching a new marketing campaign next week.
  • The marketing campaign resulted in a 30% increase in traffic.

content.text: Let's summarize the main points we've agreed on before we adjourn the meeting for {{c1::today}}.
content.explanation: To 'adjourn the meeting' is formal language meaning to temporarily end or suspend a meeting or assembly.
content.spanish: Resumamos los puntos principales en los que hemos estado de acuerdo antes de levantar la sesión por hoy.
content.usage:
Pattern: adjourn the meeting / end the session
  • The committee decided to adjourn the meeting until next Tuesday.
  • The chairman adjourned the meeting at 4:00 PM.

content.text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to the {{c1::first item}}.
content.explanation: To 'approve the minutes' is the formal process in business meetings of verifying that the written record of the previous meeting is accurate.
content.spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos al primer punto.
content.usage:
Pattern: approve the minutes / review minutes
  • The board voted to approve the minutes of the January meeting.
  • First, we must approve the minutes before discussing new business.

content.text: I'd like to invite our guest speaker to share their insights on industry trends and customer {{c1::behavior}}.
content.explanation: To 'share insights' means to explain deep understandings, professional knowledge, or analytical conclusions about a topic to others.
content.spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro orador invitado a compartir sus conocimientos/perspectivas sobre las tendencias de la industria y el comportamiento del cliente.
content.usage:
Pattern: share insights / offer recommendations
  • The analyst shared valuable insights on market growth.
  • Thank you for sharing your insights with us today.

content.text: Let's stay focused on the topic at hand; we can discuss other issues during our next scheduled {{c1::sync call}}.
content.explanation: The 'topic at hand' is the specific subject, problem, or agenda item currently being discussed or addressed.
content.spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en el tema en cuestión; podemos discutir otros asuntos durante nuestra próxima llamada de sincronización programada.
content.usage:
Pattern: topic at hand / current subject
  • Please limit your questions to the topic at hand.
  • The manager guided the team back to the topic at hand.

content.text: Thank you for your active participation today; I will send out a follow-up email with the next steps {{c1::shortly}}.
content.explanation: 'Active participation' refers to actively engaging, speaking, and contributing value during a group meeting or workshop.
content.spanish: Gracias por su participación activa hoy; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con los siguientes pasos en breve.
content.usage:
Pattern: active participation / engage in discussion
  • The success of the brainstorming session depended on everyone's active participation.
  • Thank you all for your active participation today.

content.text: Let's start the meeting on time; we have a lot of topics to cover on our agenda {{c1::today}}.
content.explanation: 'Today' refers to the present day, commonly used to set context at the start of a business call.
content.spanish: Comencemos la reunión a tiempo; tenemos muchos temas que cubrir en nuestra agenda hoy.
content.usage:
Pattern: on our agenda today / schedule for today
  • What is the main topic on our agenda today?
  • We completed all tasks scheduled for today.

content.text: I'll be leading the meeting today; please let me know if anyone has any updates to add to the {{c1::agenda}}.
content.explanation: An 'agenda' is a list of items or topics to be discussed or resolved during a formal meeting.
content.spanish: Estaré guiando la reunión hoy; por favor, avísenme si alguien tiene alguna actualización que agregar a la agenda.
content.usage:
Pattern: meeting agenda / items on the agenda
  • Please review the agenda before the call starts.
  • The manager added two new points to the meeting agenda.

content.text: Let's keep our updates brief so we have enough time to discuss the database migration {{c1::plan}}.
content.explanation: A 'plan' is a detailed proposal for doing or achieving something, here referencing the technical migration plan.
content.spanish: Mantengamos nuestras actualizaciones breves para que tengamos suficiente tiempo para discutir el plan de migración de la base de datos.
content.usage:
Pattern: migration plan / project plan
  • The architect drafted a detailed migration plan.
  • We must follow the rollout plan to avoid service disruption.

content.text: Could you please summarize your findings in two sentences to help us stay on {{c1::schedule}}?
content.explanation: To 'stay on schedule' is the business collocation meaning to complete tasks or run meetings within the planned timetable.
content.spanish: ¿Podrías por favor resumir tus hallazgos en dos oraciones para ayudarnos a mantenernos según lo programado?
content.usage:
Pattern: stay on schedule / keep on schedule
  • We adjusted the timeline to ensure we stay on schedule.
  • The project manager is responsible for keeping the team on schedule.

content.text: Let's review the action items we agreed on before we close the meeting for {{c1::this afternoon}}?
content.explanation: 'This afternoon' refers to the latter part of the present day, commonly set as a deadline or closure time for calls.
content.spanish: ¿Revisamos las tareas pendientes en las que estuvimos de acuerdo antes de cerrar la reunión de esta tarde?
content.usage:
Pattern: this afternoon / late afternoon
  • The migration is scheduled for this afternoon.
  • I will send the report this afternoon.

content.text: If there are no objections, let's approve the previous meeting minutes and move to our first {{c1::topic}}.
content.explanation: A 'topic' is a subject or theme of discussion or study in a meeting.
content.spanish: Si no hay objeciones, aprobemos la minuta de la reunión anterior y pasemos a nuestro primer tema.
content.usage:
Pattern: first topic / main topic / subject of discussion
  • The first topic on our agenda is database latency.
  • Let's move to the next topic.

content.text: I'd like to invite our product manager to present the new features scheduled for the next product {{c1::release}}.
content.explanation: A 'release' (or deployment) is the distribution of a new version of software to production.
content.spanish: Me gustaría invitar a nuestro gerente de producto a presentar las nuevas características programadas para el próximo lanzamiento del producto.
content.usage:
Pattern: product release / next release
  • We are coding the features for the next release.
  • The code is scheduled for the upcoming release.

content.text: Let's stay focused on the project milestones; we can discuss other issues in our weekly {{c1::check-in call}}.
content.explanation: A 'check-in call' (or status sync) is a regular, brief meeting to monitor progress and coordinate tasks.
content.spanish: Mantengámonos enfocados en los hitos del proyecto; podemos discutir otros asuntos en nuestra llamada de control semanal.
content.usage:
Pattern: weekly check-in call / daily check-in
  • The manager schedules a check-in call every Monday.
  • Let's use the check-in call to coordinate our updates.

content.text: Thank you all for joining; I will send out a follow-up email with the meeting notes and action items {{c1::shortly}}.
content.explanation: 'Shortly' is an adverb meaning in a short time from now; soon, indicating rapid follow-up action.
content.spanish: Gracias a todos por unirse; enviaré un correo electrónico de seguimiento con las notas de la reunión y las tareas pendientes en breve.
content.usage:
Pattern: shortly after / shortly before / arrive shortly
  • The service was restored shortly after the reboot.
  • The manager will join the call shortly.

content.text: If anyone has any remaining questions, please post them on our Slack channel to avoid running {{c1::over time}}.
content.explanation: To run 'over time' (or exceed schedule) is the phrasal verb meaning to take longer than the allocated meeting duration.
content.spanish: Si alguien tiene alguna pregunta restante, por favor publíquela en nuestro canal de Slack para evitar pasarnos de tiempo.
content.usage:
Pattern: running over time / exceed time limits
  • We must end the call now to avoid running over time.
  • The workshop ran over time by thirty minutes.

content.text: When a customer is upset about an outage, you can say: "I completely {{c1::understand how frustrating}} it must be to {{c2::have your workflow disrupted}}."
content.explanation: This phrase validates the customer's feelings and shows you understand the impact of the issue on their business, which immediately lowers tension.
content.spanish: Cuando un cliente está molesto por una interrupción, puedes decir: "Entiendo completamente lo frustrante que debe ser que se interrumpa tu flujo de trabajo".
content.usage:
Pattern: I understand how frustrating it must be to [action/situation]
  • I understand how frustrating it must be to lose access during a launch.
  • I understand how frustrating it must be to wait for this update.

content.text: To reassure a panicked client, use ownership language: "I'm going to {{c1::take ownership of this issue}} and ensure we {{c2::get this sorted out for you}} as quickly as possible."
content.explanation: Take ownership means accepting personal responsibility for finding a solution. Get sorted out is a common idiom meaning to resolve or organize a problem.
content.spanish: Para tranquilizar a un cliente en pánico, utiliza un lenguaje de propiedad: "Voy a hacerme cargo de este problema y me aseguraré de resolverlo lo antes posible".
content.usage:
Pattern: take ownership of [issue] / get [something] sorted out
  • Our team will take ownership of the database migration.
  • Don't worry, we'll get this sorted out by the end of the day.

content.scenario: Support English: Framing Bad News Positive 💡
content.text: Instead of saying "We can't do that," frame it constructively: "While that feature is {{c1::not currently supported}}, what we can do is {{c2::set up a workaround}} using our API."
content.explanation: Not currently supported is a professional way to say 'impossible' or 'no'. **Set up a workaround** means finding an alternative method to achieve the same result.
content.spanish: En lugar de decir "No podemos hacer eso", plantéalo de manera constructiva: "Aunque esa función no es compatible actualmente, lo que podemos hacer es configurar una solución alternativa utilizando nuestra API".
content.usage:
Pattern: While [request] is not currently supported, what we can do is [alternative]
  • While custom domains are not currently supported, what we can do is redirect your URL.
  • Let's set up a workaround until the patch is released.

content.text: To transition from an apology to appreciation, say: "Thank you for {{c1::bringing this to our attention}}, and I appreciate your {{c2::patience while we investigate}}."
content.explanation: Bring to attention is the standard way to acknowledge a bug report or complaint. Appreciating patience is much more positive and empowering than repeatedly saying 'sorry'.
content.spanish: Para pasar de una disculpa al agradecimiento, di: "Gracias por llamar nuestra atención sobre esto, y agradezco su paciencia mientras investigamos".
content.usage:
Pattern: Thank you for bringing [issue] to our attention / appreciate your patience while we [action]
  • Thank you for bringing this rendering bug to our attention.
  • We appreciate your patience while we deploy the fix.

content.text: I appreciate your patience while we work to resolve this issue; I understand how important this is to your {{c1::business operations}}.
content.explanation: 'Business operations' refers to the core activities, systems, and processes that a company executes daily to deliver value and generate revenue.
content.spanish: Agradezco su paciencia mientras trabajamos para resolver este problema; entiendo lo importante que es esto para sus operaciones comerciales.
content.usage:
Pattern: business operations / day-to-day operations
  • A major database crash can disrupt critical business operations.
  • We must resume business operations as quickly as possible.

content.text: I apologize for the inconvenience this has caused you, and I am committed to making this right as {{c1::quickly as possible}}.
content.explanation: 'As quickly as possible' (or ASAP) is a common phrase indicating that a task should be executed with the maximum speed and urgency.
content.spanish: Le pido disculpas por el inconveniente que esto le ha causado, y me comprometo a corregir esto tan rápido como sea posible.
content.usage:
Pattern: as quickly as possible / with high urgency
  • Please restore the website connection as quickly as possible.
  • We will process your refund as quickly as possible.

content.text: I hear your concerns, and I will escalate this ticket to our senior engineering team for immediate {{c1::attention}}.
content.explanation: 'Immediate attention' refers to high-priority focus and action required immediately to resolve a critical issue or complaint.
content.spanish: Escucho sus preocupaciones, y escalaré este ticket a nuestro equipo de ingeniería sénior para su atención inmediata.
content.usage:
Pattern: requires immediate attention / top priority
  • The security breach requires immediate attention from the director.
  • We will give this matter our immediate attention.

content.text: Please rest assured that we are taking this matter very seriously and will keep you updated every step of the {{c1::way}}.
content.explanation: To keep someone updated 'every step of the way' means to provide regular, detailed updates at each milestone or phase of a process.
content.spanish: Tenga la seguridad de que nos estamos tomando este asunto muy en serio y le mantendremos informado en cada paso del camino.
content.usage:
Pattern: every step of the way / continuous updates
  • Our support team will guide you every step of the way during migration.
  • We will keep you informed every step of the way.

content.text: I know this is frustrating, but I will stay on the line with you until we verify that your connection is {{c1::fully restored}}.
content.explanation: To be 'fully restored' means that a system or network connection has returned to 100% capacity, stability, and functionality after an outage.
content.spanish: Sé que esto es frustrante, pero permaneceré en la línea con usted hasta que verifiquemos que su conexión está totalmente restaurada.
content.usage:
Pattern: fully restored / connection restored
  • The database backup is complete, and services are fully restored.
  • We will not close the ticket until service is fully restored.

content.text: Thank you for bringing this to our attention; your feedback helps us improve our service for {{c1::all our customers}}.
content.explanation: To 'bring [something] to someone's attention' means to notify them or make them aware of a problem, mistake, or issue.
content.spanish: Gracias por llamar nuestra atención sobre esto; sus comentarios nos ayudan a mejorar nuestro servicio para todos nuestros clientes.
content.usage:
Pattern: bring to attention / point out a bug
  • Thank you for bringing this calculation error to our attention.
  • He brought the database memory leak to my attention.

content.text: I understand how critical this issue is to your business operations, and I will do my best to {{c1::resolve it quickly}}.
content.explanation: To 'resolve it quickly' means solving a technical bug or customer ticket with high speed and priority.
content.spanish: Entiendo lo crítico que es este problema para sus operaciones comerciales, y haré todo lo posible para resolverlo rápidamente.
content.usage:
Pattern: resolve it quickly / fast resolution
  • The support team is working hard to resolve the database crash quickly.
  • We must address the server issue and resolve it quickly.

content.text: I apologize for the frustration this outage has caused; we are working around the clock to restore the {{c1::service}}.
content.explanation: To 'restore service' means bringing a software system, network, or server database back online after a crash.
content.spanish: Pido disculpas por la frustración que esta interrupción ha causado; estamos trabajando las 24 horas del día para restablecer el servicio.
content.usage:
Pattern: restore service / restore database / back online
  • The hosting company worked for hours to restore service.
  • We restored the network service after replacing the switch.

content.text: I hear your concerns about data security, and I will escalate this ticket to our security team for immediate {{c1::review}}.
content.explanation: A 'review' is a formal assessment of something with the intention of instituting changes if necessary (e.g. security log analysis).
content.spanish: Escucho sus preocupaciones sobre la seguridad de los datos, y escalaré este ticket a nuestro equipo de seguridad para una revisión inmediata.
content.usage:
Pattern: under review / security review / code review
  • The security incident is currently under review.
  • We scheduled a review of our server access policies.

content.text: Please rest assured that we are investigating this matter thoroughly and will keep you informed every step of the {{c1::way}}.
content.explanation: To 'keep someone informed' means providing regular updates to a client or stakeholder during an incident investigation.
content.spanish: Tenga la seguridad de que estamos investigando este asunto a fondo y le mantendremos informado en cada paso del camino.
content.usage:
Pattern: keep you informed / regular status updates
  • I will keep you informed of any database schema modifications.
  • We kept the client informed during the server migration.

content.text: I know this is inconvenient, but I will stay on the call with you until we verify that your system is {{c1::fully operational}}.
content.explanation: To be 'fully operational' means that a software platform or server is running at 100% capacity, without any bugs or latency.
content.spanish: Sé que esto es inconveniente, pero permaneceré en la llamada con usted hasta que verifiquemos que su sistema está totalmente operativo.
content.usage:
Pattern: fully operational / fully functional / active
  • The billing system is now fully operational.
  • We tested the database and verified it is fully operational.

content.text: Thank you for bringing this calculation error to our attention; we will fix it in our next update {{c1::patch}}.
content.explanation: A 'patch' is a quick update or set of code changes designed to fix a bug or patch a security vulnerability in a software release.
content.spanish: Gracias por llamar nuestra atención sobre este error de cálculo; lo corregiremos en nuestro próximo parche de actualización.
content.usage:
Pattern: deploy a patch / security patch
  • We are deploying a security patch to fix the API leak.
  • The patch resolved several display glitches.

content.text: I want to make sure we resolve this issue to your satisfaction; please let me know if there is anything else I can {{c1::do for you}}.
content.explanation: To 'do for you' is the natural customer service phrase asking if there are any other problems or tickets that need attention.
content.spanish: Quiero asegurarme de que resolvamos este problema a su satisfacción; por favor, avíseme si hay algo más que pueda hacer por usted.
content.usage:
Pattern: do for you / help you with
  • Is there anything else I can do for you today, sir?
  • Please let me know what I can do for you to resolve this.

content.text: I appreciate your patience while we test the connection; it should be ready in less than {{c1::five minutes}}.
content.explanation: 'Five minutes' is a short time reference used to reassure customers that a technical operation is almost complete.
content.spanish: Agradezco su paciencia mientras probamos la conexión; debería estar lista en menos de cinco minutos.
content.usage:
Pattern: in five minutes / five minutes remaining
  • The server reboot will complete in five minutes.
  • We have five minutes remaining on this call.

content.text: I apologize for the delay in our response; our support team has been experiencing a high volume of {{c1::tickets today}}.
content.explanation: A 'high volume of tickets' (or high call volume) means that the customer support queue is receiving more requests than usual, causing delays.
content.spanish: Pido disculpas por la demora en nuestra respuesta; nuestro equipo de soporte ha estado experimentando un alto volumen de tickets hoy.
content.usage:
Pattern: high volume of tickets / call volume spike
  • Due to the server outage, we are experiencing a high volume of tickets today.
  • Our response times are longer due to a high volume of tickets.

content.text: Your satisfaction is our top priority; please let us know if the temporary workaround resolves your problem {{c1::for now}}.
content.explanation: 'For now' is a common temporal expression meaning temporarily, at the moment, or until a permanent fix is ready.
content.spanish: Su satisfacción es nuestra máxima prioridad; por favor, háganos saber si la solución temporal resuelve su problema por ahora.
content.usage:
Pattern: for now / temporarily / until then
  • Use this temporary password for now.
  • The server is stable for now, but we need to monitor it.

content.text: When a customer's architectural design is flawed, you can push back gracefully by saying: "While your setup is functional, it {{c1::introduces a single point of failure}} that could {{c2::compromise high availability}} during peak traffic."
content.explanation: Pointing out design risks (like single points of failure) using objective technical concepts rather than telling the client they are 'wrong' avoids defensiveness and builds advisory trust.
content.spanish: Cuando el diseño arquitectónico de un cliente es defectuoso, puedes retroceder con gracia diciendo: "Aunque su configuración es funcional, introduce un punto único de falla que podría comprometer la alta disponibilidad durante los picos de tráfico".
content.usage:
Pattern: [Setup] introduces [risk] that could compromise [performance metric]
  • This setup introduces a single point of failure that could compromise high availability.
  • Using a single DB instance introduces a bottleneck that could compromise throughput.

content.text: To handle a client demanding a new capability as a bug fix, say: "This behavior {{c1::aligns with current system design}}; however, I will {{c2::advocate for your use case}} directly with our product team."
content.explanation: "Aligns with current system design" is a professional way to state that the system is not broken, while "advocate for your use case" shows you are still partner-focused.
content.spanish: Para manejar a un cliente que exige una nueva capacidad como si fuera una corrección de errores, di: "Este comportamiento se alinea con el diseño actual del sistema; sin embargo, defenderé su caso de uso directamente con nuestro equipo de producto".
content.usage:
Pattern: [Behavior] aligns with current system design; however, I will advocate for [use case]
  • The login timeout aligns with current system design; however, I will advocate for your use case of extending it.
  • Not allowing bulk deletions aligns with current system design.

content.text: In a Post-Mortem or Root Cause Analysis (RCA), always close by saying: "To prevent recurrence, we are {{c1::implementing automated safeguards}} and {{c2::refining our circuit breaker thresholds}}."
content.explanation: An RCA must culminate in concrete, systemic preventive actions. Explaining the safeguards demonstrates that the organization has learned and adapted to protect the client's operations.
content.spanish: En un análisis de causa raíz (RCA), siempre cierra diciendo: "Para evitar que vuelva a ocurrir, estamos implementando salvaguardas automatizadas y refinando los límites de nuestros interruptores automáticos".
content.usage:
Pattern: To prevent recurrence, we are implementing [preventative action]
  • To prevent recurrence, we are implementing automated safeguards and database limits.
  • We are refining our circuit breaker thresholds to prevent cascade failures.

content.text: To politely refuse debugging custom scripts, say: "While I can confirm our API is responding normally, {{c1::custom scripts are outside our support scope}}; however, you can reference this {{c2::sample payload}}."
content.explanation: Clearly states the platform's boundaries (outside our support scope) but remains helpful by providing a reference example to guide the customer.
content.spanish: Para rechazar cortésmente la depuración de scripts personalizados, di: "Aunque puedo confirmar que nuestra API está respondiendo normalmente, los scripts personalizados están fuera de nuestro alcance de soporte; sin embargo, puede consultar este ejemplo de carga útil".
content.usage:
Pattern: [Topic] is outside our support scope; however, you can reference [alternative resource]
  • Debugging custom scripts is outside our support scope; however, you can reference our sample repository.
  • Third-party libraries are outside our support scope.

content.text: Instead of apologizing for a delay, use appreciation to stay assertive: "Thank you for your patience while we {{c1::conducted a deep-dive review}} into your {{c2::infrastructure configuration}}."
content.explanation: Saying 'thank you for your patience' validates the wait while asserting that the time was spent on a high-value action (deep-dive review), preserving authority.
content.spanish: En lugar de disculparse por un retraso, use el agradecimiento para mantener la asertividad: "Gracias por su paciencia mientras realizábamos una revisión profunda de la configuración de su infraestructura".
content.usage:
Pattern: Thank you for your patience while we conducted a deep-dive review into [infrastructure/database/setup]
  • Thank you for your patience while we conducted a deep-dive review into your replication logs.
  • Our team conducted a deep-dive review and resolved the memory leak.

content.text: To deflect a premature escalation to management, suggest: "I want to ensure we {{c1::exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths}} before we {{c2::engage our engineering lead}}, so we can present them with complete data."
content.explanation: This phrase positions the delay in escalation as a benefit to the customer (ensuring they have complete data first), maintaining control of the troubleshooting flow.
content.spanish: Para desviar una escalación prematura a la gerencia, sugiera: "Quiero asegurarme de que agotemos todas las vías de diagnóstico inmediato antes de involucrar a nuestro líder de ingeniería, para que podamos presentarles datos completos".
content.usage:
Pattern: exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before we engage [lead/expert]
  • I want to ensure we exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before we engage our engineering lead.
  • Let's exhaust all immediate diagnostic paths before escalation.

content.text: To admit an error without losing credibility, state: "Our initial assessment {{c1::missed X variable}}; we have {{c2::adjusted our troubleshooting path}} to focus on Y to get you back online safely."
content.explanation: Directly acknowledges the new information (missed variable) and immediately pivots to the updated action plan, projecting competence and responsiveness rather than defensiveness.
content.spanish: Para admitir un error sin perder credibilidad, declare: "Nuestra evaluación inicial pasó por alto la variable X; hemos ajustado nuestro camino de resolución de problemas para enfocarnos en Y para que vuelva a estar en línea de manera segura".
content.usage:
Pattern: Our initial assessment missed [variable]; we have adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on [focus]
  • Our initial assessment missed the firewall block; we have adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on security rules.
  • We adjusted our troubleshooting path to focus on replication delays.

content.text: To pivot a call when time is running out, say: "We have 15 minutes left on our scheduled block. To make sure we {{c1::lock down next steps}} for your production deployment, let's {{c2::pivot to the action items}}."
content.explanation: Using "lock down next steps" and "pivot to action items" focuses the remaining time on outcomes, preventing the meeting from ending without clear deliverables.
content.spanish: Para reorientar una llamada cuando se acaba el tiempo, di: "Nos quedan 15 minutos en nuestro bloque programado. Para asegurarnos de acordar los próximos pasos para su implementación en producción, pasemos a los elementos de acción".
content.usage:
Pattern: We have [time] left on our scheduled block. To make sure we lock down next steps for [goal], let's pivot to [action items]
  • We have 15 minutes left on our scheduled block. To make sure we lock down next steps for your production migration, let's pivot to the action items.
  • Let's make sure we lock down next steps before the meeting ends.

content.text: To politely interrupt a client repeating the same complaint: "I hear you, and to ensure we don't {{c1::lose ground on the diagnostic work}}, let's focus on collecting the server logs."
content.explanation: "Lose ground on" means to lose progress already made. This redirect politely signals that repeating the complaint stops active troubleshooting.
content.spanish: Para interrumpir cortésmente a un cliente que repite la misma queja: "Lo escucho, y para asegurarnos de no perder terreno en el trabajo de diagnóstico, concentrémonos en recopilar los registros del servidor".
content.usage:
Pattern: I hear you, and to ensure we don't lose ground on [work/diagnostics], let's focus on [next step]
  • I hear you, and to ensure we don't lose ground on the diagnostic work, let's focus on collecting the server logs.
  • We don't want to lose ground on the replication analysis.

content.text: We need to conduct a Root Cause Analysis (RCA) to determine why the database crashed during the {{c1::peak traffic hour}}.
content.explanation: 'Peak traffic hour' refers to the specific time of day when a web service or network receives the highest volume of users or transactions.
content.spanish: Necesitamos realizar un Análisis de Causa Raíz (RCA) para determinar por qué la base de datos colapsó durante la hora de mayor tráfico.
content.usage:
Pattern: peak traffic / high load times
  • Our infrastructure must scale up automatically during peak traffic.
  • Latency spikes occur mostly during peak traffic hours.

content.text: We need to conduct a post-mortem analysis to determine the root cause of the memory leak in our production {{c1::servers}}.
content.explanation: A 'production server' (or prod server) is the live server hosting the application active for real users, distinct from staging or test servers.
content.spanish: Necesitamos realizar un análisis posterior al incidente (post-mortem) para determinar la causa raíz de la fuga de memoria en nuestros servidores de producción.
content.usage:
Pattern: production servers / deploy to production
  • Do not run untested scripts directly on production servers.
  • The production servers crashed due to high CPU load.

content.text: Our immediate focus is to apply a hotfix to restore service, then work on a long-term {{c1::architectural fix}}.
content.explanation: An 'architectural fix' is a fundamental redesign of database schemas, code modules, or network topologies to resolve a recurring issue permanently.
content.spanish: Nuestro enfoque inmediato es aplicar un parche para restablecer el servicio y luego trabajar en una corrección arquitectónica a largo plazo.
content.usage:
Pattern: architectural fix / permanent redesign
  • Applying a temporary workaround is not an alternative to an architectural fix.
  • The architect proposed a database partitioning model as the architectural fix.

content.text: The crash was caused by a database deadlock; we need to optimize our queries and transaction isolation {{c1::levels}}.
content.explanation: 'Transaction isolation levels' in SQL databases control the visibility of data to other concurrent transactions, preventing data corruption.
content.spanish: El colapso fue causado por un bloqueo mutuo (deadlock) de la base de datos; necesitamos optimizar nuestras consultas y niveles de aislamiento de transacciones.
content.usage:
Pattern: isolation levels / read committed / serializable
  • Setting high transaction isolation levels can increase locks and cause deadlocks.
  • We set the database isolation level to Read Committed.

content.text: Let's review the API response times to identify when the performance degradation began to {{c1::affect users}}.
content.explanation: To 'affect users' means that software bugs, latency, or outages begin to disrupt the client experience or cause errors on user devices.
content.spanish: Revisemos los tiempos de respuesta de la API para identificar cuándo la degradación del rendimiento comenzó a afectar a los usuarios.
content.usage:
Pattern: affect users / impact clients
  • The database outage did not affect users because of the fallback cache.
  • How many clients did the latency spike affect?

content.text: We need to negotiate the SLA details: what is the penalty if the uptime drops below the guaranteed {{c1::99.9%}}?
content.explanation: '99.9%' (three nines) is a standard service level agreement uptime metric, permitting no more than 8.76 hours of downtime per year.
content.spanish: Necesitamos negociar los detalles del SLA: ¿cuál es la penalización si el tiempo de actividad cae por debajo del 99.9% garantizado?
content.usage:
Pattern: 99.9% uptime / 99.99% availability
  • The hosting provider committed to a 99.9% uptime guarantee.
  • Our service fell below the 99.9% threshold this month.

content.text: The post-mortem report indicates that the outage could have been avoided if we had set up server resource monitoring {{c1::alerts}}.
content.explanation: A 'monitoring alert' is an automated notification (sent via Slack, PagerDuty, or email) triggered when system metrics (like CPU or memory) cross a threshold.
content.spanish: El informe posterior al incidente indica que la interrupción podría haberse evitado si hubiéramos configurado alertas de monitoreo de recursos del servidor.
content.usage:
Pattern: monitoring alerts / trigger an alert
  • Configure monitoring alerts to notify the team when disk space is low.
  • The database memory leak triggered several monitoring alerts.

content.text: Let's establish a roll-back plan before we deploy this update to production to minimize potential service {{c1::disruptions}}.
content.explanation: 'Service disruptions' are events where software systems or cloud networks become unavailable, causing operations to halt.
content.spanish: Establezcamos un plan de reversión antes de implementar esta actualización en producción para minimizar posibles interrupciones del servicio.
content.usage:
Pattern: service disruptions / business disruption
  • The network migration caused minimal service disruptions.
  • Avoid service disruptions by testing all updates in staging first.

content.text: We need to run load tests to verify that the API gateway can handle up to double our normal request {{c1::volume}}.
content.explanation: 'Request volume' refers to the total number of HTTP requests processed by a web server or gateway within a specific timeframe.
content.spanish: Necesitamos realizar pruebas de carga para verificar que la pasarela API pueda manejar hasta el doble de nuestro volumen de solicitudes normal.
content.usage:
Pattern: request volume / traffic volume / concurrent requests
  • Our API gateway handles a high request volume during marketing events.
  • The server crashed because the request volume exceeded its limits.

content.text: The root cause was a configuration mismatch between the staging and production environment {{c1::variables}}.
content.explanation: 'Environment variables' are dynamic-valued objects stored in the operating system that define configuration values (like DB paths, keys) for running processes.
content.spanish: La causa raíz fue una discrepancia de configuración entre las variables de entorno de los entornos de prueba (staging) y producción.
content.usage:
Pattern: environment variables / config variables
  • Make sure you do not commit environment variables containing secrets.
  • The app crashed because the database URL environment variable was missing.

content.text: We will document our findings and remediation steps in a formal RCA report and share it with the client {{c1::tomorrow}}.
content.explanation: 'Tomorrow' is the day after today, set here as the target deadline to share the Root Cause Analysis report with stakeholders.
content.spanish: Documentaremos nuestros hallazgos y pasos de remediación en un informe formal de RCA y lo compartiremos con el cliente mañana.
content.usage:
Pattern: share it tomorrow / report tomorrow
  • The slides are ready; we will share the report tomorrow.
  • I will email you the updates tomorrow.

content.text: Could you help me with this quickly? I’m a bit {{c1::stuck}}.
content.explanation: Is a very natural colloquial word used when you need help or are blocked on a task. It sounds more conversational than 'I am having difficulty'
content.spanish: ¿Podrías ayudarme con esto rápidamente? Estoy un poco atascado.
content.usage:
Pattern: I’m stuck / could you help me?
  • I’m stuck on this part of the report.
  • Could you help me with this quickly?

content.text: Just to {{c1::clarify}}, do you want this done by noon or by 3 PM?
content.explanation: Is a useful phrase for confirming expectations clearly and professionally
content.spanish: Solo para aclararlo, ¿quieres que esto esté listo al mediodía o a las 3 PM?
content.usage:
Pattern: Just to clarify
  • Just to clarify, should I send it today?
  • Just to clarify, what’s the priority here?

content.text: I just wanted to give you a quick {{c1::update}} on where things stand.
content.explanation: This phrase is very common in work conversations when you want to share progress briefly.
content.spanish: Solo quería darte una actualización rápida sobre el estado de las cosas.
content.usage:
Pattern: give you a quick update
  • I wanted to give you a quick update before the meeting.
  • Here is the latest update.

content.text: So if we move this to Friday, we’re all {{c1::on the same page}}.
content.explanation: To be on the same page means that everyone understands and agrees on the same plan or idea.
content.spanish: Entonces si movemos esto al viernes, todos estamos en la misma página.
content.usage:
Pattern: on the same page
  • We need to be on the same page before launch.
  • Are we all on the same page?

content.text: Can we aim to have this wrapped up by {{c1::Friday}}?
content.explanation: Wrapped up means completed. This is a natural workplace phrase for discussing deadlines.
content.spanish: ¿Podemos pretender tener esto listo para el viernes?
content.usage:
Pattern: wrapped up / aim to have it done by
  • Can we have it wrapped up by Monday?
  • We aim to have the draft done by Friday.

content.text: Would it be possible to get a short {{c1::extension}} on this?
content.explanation: An extension means extra time to finish something. It is a common workplace request.
content.spanish: ¿Sería posible conseguir una pequeña extensión para esto?
content.usage:
Pattern: get an extension
  • Could I get an extension on the deadline?
  • We need a short extension.

content.text: I’m following up on my earlier message about the {{c1::timeline}}.
content.explanation: Following up is a standard phrase for checking on a previous request or email.
content.spanish: Estoy haciendo seguimiento a mi mensaje anterior sobre el calendario.
content.usage:
Pattern: following up on
  • I’m following up on the document.
  • Just following up on our last conversation.

content.text: I hear what you’re saying, but I’d like to {{c1::push back}} a bit on that proposal.
content.explanation: To push back means to respectfully disagree or challenge an idea. It is a useful professional phrase.
content.spanish: Te oigo, pero me gustaría objetar un poco esa propuesta.
content.usage:
Pattern: push back on / push back against
  • I’d like to push back on that recommendation.
  • We need to push back on the timeline.
